          card(min_height = "400px", card_header("File Growth (6-Month Rolling)"), uiOutput("rh_file_growth"),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small", "6-month rolling aggregate of files added vs deleted — green = additions, red = deletions"))
        )
      ),
      nav_panel("Contributors & Activity",
        layout_columns(col_widths = c(6, 6),
          card(min_height = "400px", card_header("Bus Factor (All Time)"), uiOutput("rh_bus_all"),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small", "Contributor commit share — 1 person at 60%+ = risk")),
          card(min_height = "400px", card_header("Bus Factor (6 Months)"), uiOutput("rh_bus_6mo"),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small", "Recent contributor concentration"))
        ),
        layout_columns(col_widths = c(12),
          card(min_height = "400px", card_header("GitHub Issue Activity (Last 30 Days)"), uiOutput("gh_events_chart"),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small", "Issue open/close/label events across tracked repos"))
        )
      ),
      nav_panel("Code Health",
        layout_columns(col_widths = c(6, 6),
          card(min_height = "400px", card_header("Churn Hotspots"), uiOutput("rh_churn"),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small", "Most-modified files in last 6 months")),
          card(min_height = "400px", card_header("Bug Hotspots"), uiOutput("rh_bugs"),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small", "Files most often touched by fix/bug commits"))
        ),
        layout_columns(col_widths = c(6, 6),
          card(min_height = "400px", card_header("TODO/FIXME Debt"), DTOutput("rh_todo"),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small", "Technical debt markers by file")),
          card(min_height = "400px", card_header("Crisis Commits"), DTOutput("rh_crisis"),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small", "Reverts, hotfixes, and emergency commits (last year)"))
        ),
        layout_columns(col_widths = c(12),
          card(min_height = "400px", card_header("File Churn Hotspots (Phase 2)"), DTOutput("file_churn_table"),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small", "Top files by lines changed over the last year, by project. Hotspots = high-risk surface area."))
        ),
        layout_columns(col_widths = c(12),
          card(min_height = "400px", card_header("Change Coupling Graph (Phase 2)"), uiOutput("change_coupling_graph"),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small", "Files that frequently change together. Hover an edge to see co-change count. Highly-coupled clusters suggest hidden module boundaries. Filter by Repo in sidebar."))
        )
      ),
      nav_panel("Reviews", value = "Reviews",
        # 18 cards across 5 sub-tabs — grouped semantically. The
        # shown.bs.tab handler at L741 already re-renders mermaid + echarts
        # when sub-tabs come into view, so the cost of stacking is paid
        # only when the reader actually navigates to that group.
        navset_tab(
        nav_panel("Pulse",
        layout_columns(col_widths = c(12),
          card(
            card_header("Roborev — Automated Code Review Health"),
            tags$div(class = "p-3", style = "color: #e0e0e0; font-size: 0.9em;",
              tags$p(
                "The roborev pipeline runs ", tags$code("critic"), " and ",
                tags$code("fixer"), " agents on every commit, then tracks whether
                findings recur across review rounds. Loops = findings fixed and
                re-opened more than once. Data sourced from ",
                tags$code("unified.duckdb"), ". For the full interactive summary ",
                "see the ",
                tags$a(href = "roborev_summary.html", target = "_blank",
                       style = "color:#7cb5ec;", "roborev_summary vignette"),
                " or the detailed ",
                tags$a(href = "roborev_resolution.html", target = "_blank",
                       style = "color:#7cb5ec;", "Roborev Resolution page"), "."
              )
            ),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small",
              "Roborev pipeline health summary. Data refreshed on each tar_make() run.")
          )
        ),
        layout_columns(col_widths = c(12),
          card(
            card_header("Roborev Pulse"),
            tags$table(class = "table table-sm table-dark mb-0",
              tags$thead(tags$tr(tags$th("Metric"), tags$th("Value"))),
              tags$tbody(
                tags$tr(tags$td("Open findings"), tags$td(textOutput("rv_n_open", inline = TRUE))),
                tags$tr(tags$td("  Critical"),    tags$td(textOutput("rv_n_critical", inline = TRUE))),
                tags$tr(tags$td("  High"),         tags$td(textOutput("rv_n_high", inline = TRUE))),
                tags$tr(tags$td("Resolved"),       tags$td(textOutput("rv_n_resolved", inline = TRUE))),
                tags$tr(tags$td("Active loops"),   tags$td(textOutput("rv_n_loops", inline = TRUE))),
                tags$tr(tags$td("  Escalate (no ack)"),
                        tags$td(textOutput("rv_n_escalate", inline = TRUE))),
                tags$tr(tags$td("Est. wasted USD"),
                        tags$td(textOutput("rv_wasted_usd", inline = TRUE)))
              )
            ),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small",
              "Counts sourced from unified.duckdb roborev_findings and roborev_loops tables.")
          )
        ),
        layout_columns(col_widths = c(12),
          card(card_header("High-Severity Findings by Repo"),
            uiOutput("rv_by_repo_chart"),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small",
              "Cleveland dot chart showing the number of high-severity roborev findings per repository, ",
              "sorted descending by finding count. Each dot represents one repository; the x-axis shows ",
              "the total high-severity finding count (n_high from roborev_by_repo.json). ",
              "Data sourced from the roborev pipeline export — refreshed on each tar_make() run. ",
              "Chart is hidden gracefully when roborev_by_repo.json is not yet available."))
        ),
        layout_columns(col_widths = c(12),
          card(card_header("Active Loops"), DTOutput("rv_loops_table"),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small",
              "Recurring review loops. Row colour: red=escalate, orange=block, yellow=watch. Sorted by cycles descending."))
        )
        ),  # close Pulse nav_panel
        nav_panel("Quality",
        layout_columns(col_widths = c(12),
          card(
            card_header("Resolution Rate (#146 Q5)"),
            tags$table(class = "table table-sm table-dark mb-2",
              tags$thead(tags$tr(tags$th("Metric"), tags$th("Value"))),
              tags$tbody(
                tags$tr(tags$td("Resolution rate"), tags$td(textOutput("rv_res_rate", inline = TRUE))),
                tags$tr(tags$td("Resolved (public repos)"), tags$td(textOutput("rv_res_resolved", inline = TRUE))),
                tags$tr(tags$td("Open (public repos)"),     tags$td(textOutput("rv_res_open", inline = TRUE))),
                tags$tr(tags$td("Total reviews"),           tags$td(textOutput("rv_res_total", inline = TRUE)))
              )
            ),
            uiOutput("rv_resolution_trend"),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small",
              "Resolution rate = close_reason='manual' / total, computed from roborev_resolution.json. ",
              "Confidential repos (mycare, crypto, swarms) are excluded via clean_projects() before aggregation. ",
              "The weekly trend shows resolved vs open reviews per week (based on created_at). ",
              "Data sourced from ~/.claude/logs/unified.duckdb roborev_review_lifecycle table; ",
              "refreshed on each local export run and committed to inst/extdata/ for CI serving.")
          )
        ),
        layout_columns(col_widths = c(12),
          card(
            card_header("Agent Pass Rate (#146 Q10)"),
            uiOutput("rv_agent_perf_chart"),
            tags$table(class = "table table-sm table-dark mt-2 mb-0",
              tags$thead(tags$tr(
                tags$th("Agent"), tags$th("Reviews"), tags$th("Pass rate"),
                tags$th("Pass"), tags$th("Fail"), tags$th("Error"),
                tags$th("Cost/review")
              )),
              uiOutput("rv_agent_perf_table")
            ),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small",
              "Cleveland dot chart: pass rate (pass_count / n_runs) per review agent. ",
              "Agents sourced from roborev_agent_performance in unified.duckdb (no repo column — no confidential filtering needed). ",
              "Cost per review is shown only when total_cost_usd is available; NA values display as —. ",
              "Data refreshed on each local export run and committed to inst/extdata/ for CI serving.")
          )
        ),
        layout_columns(col_widths = c(12),
          card(
            card_header("Review Backlog Age (#146 Q15)"),
            tags$table(class = "table table-sm table-dark mb-2",
              tags$thead(tags$tr(tags$th("Metric"), tags$th("Value"))),
              tags$tbody(
                tags$tr(tags$td("Open reviews"),   tags$td(textOutput("rv_ba_n_open",   inline = TRUE))),
                tags$tr(tags$td("Median age"),      tags$td(textOutput("rv_ba_median",   inline = TRUE))),
                tags$tr(tags$td("90th pct age"),    tags$td(textOutput("rv_ba_p90",      inline = TRUE))),
                tags$tr(tags$td("Oldest review"),   tags$td(textOutput("rv_ba_max",      inline = TRUE)))
              )
            ),
            uiOutput("rv_ba_chart"),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small",
              "Horizontal bar chart (Cleveland-style): number of open reviews per age bucket ",
              "(0-1d / 1-3d / 3-7d / 7-14d / >14d). ",
              "Age = now minus enqueued_at from ~/.roborev/reviews.db. ",
              "Confidential repos (mycare, crypto, solwatch, swarms) excluded. ",
              "Source: roborev_backlog_age.json — refreshed on each local export run and committed to inst/extdata/.")
          )
        ),
        layout_columns(col_widths = c(12),
          card(
            card_header("Signal-to-Noise by Repo (#146 Q3)"),
            tags$div(class = "p-3", style = "color: #e0e0e0; font-size: 0.9em;",
              tags$p(
                "One row per repository. ",
                tags$strong("Signal % "),
                "= passed reviews ÷ created reviews (higher is better). ",
                tags$strong("Noise % "),
                "= failed reviews ÷ created reviews (lower is better). ",
                "Signal + Noise + autoclosed-share = 100% for each repo. ",
                "The Cleveland dot chart ranks repos by Signal %; the table beneath gives exact counts."
              )
            ),
            uiOutput("rv_repo_signal_chart"),
            tags$table(class = "table table-sm table-dark mt-2 mb-0",
              tags$thead(tags$tr(
                tags$th(title = "Repository name (canonical_project)", "Repo"),
                tags$th(title = "Signal % = n Passed / n Created — higher is better",
                        "Signal %"),
                tags$th(title = "Noise % = n Failed / n Created — lower is better",
                        "Noise %"),
                tags$th(title = "Total reviews opened for this repo in the window",
                        "n Created"),
                tags$th(title = "Reviews that produced an actionable fix (close_reason = 'manual')",
                        "n Passed"),
                tags$th(title = "Reviews closed without action (autoclosed or rejected)",
                        "n Failed"),
                tags$th(title = "Reviews suppressed by pipeline thresholds before reaching a human reviewer",
                        "n Autoclosed")
              )),
              uiOutput("rv_repo_signal_table")
            ),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small",
              "Compared against the other repos shown — no absolute target rate. ",
              "A repo with Signal=24% and 538 created is producing ~131 actionable findings but generating ~382 noise reviews — large absolute volume but high false-positive cost. ",
              "Autoclosed = reviews suppressed by the pipeline (age/severity thresholds) before human review. ",
              "Source: roborev_daily_metrics table in unified.duckdb, aggregated across all dates. ",
              "Confidential repos (mycare, crypto, swarms) are excluded via clean_projects(). ",
              "Data refreshed on each local export run and committed to inst/extdata/ for CI serving.")
          )
        ),
        layout_columns(col_widths = c(12),
          card(
            card_header("Review Noise Rate (#146 Q2)"),
            uiOutput("rv_noise_rate_chart"),
            tags$table(class = "table table-sm table-dark mt-2 mb-0",
              tags$thead(tags$tr(
                tags$th("Metric"), tags$th("Count"), tags$th("Share")
              )),
              uiOutput("rv_noise_rate_table")
            ),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small",
              "Stacked bar (daily): review outcome segments — ",
              tags$b("passed"), " (actionable finding, fixer ran); ",
              tags$b("actioned-fail"), " (finding surfaced but fixer did not resolve it); ",
              tags$b("autoclosed-fail"), " (suppressed by age/severity threshold before human review); ",
              tags$b("parse-fail"), " (review crashed — no usable finding). ",
              "Noise pct = (actioned-fail + autoclosed-fail + parse-fail) / total. ",
              "Source: roborev_daily_metrics in unified.duckdb, aggregated across all repos per day. ",
              "Confidential repos excluded via clean_projects(). ",
              "Data refreshed on each local export run and committed to inst/extdata/ for CI serving.")
          )
        )
        ),  # close Quality nav_panel
        nav_panel("Operations",
        layout_columns(col_widths = c(12),
          card(
            card_header("Medium-Threshold Suppression (#146 Q7)"),
            uiOutput("rv_suppression_chart"),
            tags$table(class = "table table-sm table-dark mt-2 mb-0",
              tags$thead(tags$tr(
                tags$th("Repo"), tags$th("Suppressed"), tags$th("Created"),
                tags$th("Suppress %")
              )),
              uiOutput("rv_suppression_table")
            ),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small",
              "Cumulative count of findings suppressed by the medium-severity autoclose threshold ",
              "(llm#224, active since 2026-05-21). ",
              "Bar chart: top-5 repos by autoclosed_severity count. ",
              "Source: roborev_daily_metrics.reviews_autoclosed_severity in unified.duckdb. ",
              "Data filtered to dates >= 2026-05-21 when the feature went live. ",
              "Confidential repos excluded via clean_projects().")
          )
        ),
        layout_columns(col_widths = c(12),
          card(
            card_header("Cadence Reduction Effect (#146 Q6)"),
            tags$div(class = "p-3", style = "color: #e0e0e0; font-size: 0.9em;",
              tags$p(
                "Split at ", tags$strong("2026-05-21"), " (llm#217 cadence reduction date). ",
                "Before: high-frequency polling. After: reduced cadence with severity-threshold ",
                "auto-close active. Metrics: daily review volume, pass rate, and autoclosed count."
              )
            ),
            tags$table(class = "table table-sm table-dark mb-2",
              tags$thead(tags$tr(
                tags$th("Period"), tags$th("Created"), tags$th("Passed"),
                tags$th("Pass rate"), tags$th("Autoclosed"), tags$th("Days")
              )),
              tags$tbody(
                tags$tr(
                  tags$td("Before 2026-05-21"),
                  tags$td(textOutput("rv_ce_before_created",  inline = TRUE)),
                  tags$td(textOutput("rv_ce_before_passed",   inline = TRUE)),
                  tags$td(textOutput("rv_ce_before_rate",     inline = TRUE)),
                  tags$td(textOutput("rv_ce_before_autoclosed", inline = TRUE)),
                  tags$td(textOutput("rv_ce_before_days",     inline = TRUE))
                ),
                tags$tr(
                  tags$td("After 2026-05-21"),
                  tags$td(textOutput("rv_ce_after_created",   inline = TRUE)),
                  tags$td(textOutput("rv_ce_after_passed",    inline = TRUE)),
                  tags$td(textOutput("rv_ce_after_rate",      inline = TRUE)),
                  tags$td(textOutput("rv_ce_after_autoclosed", inline = TRUE)),
                  tags$td(textOutput("rv_ce_after_days",      inline = TRUE))
                )
              )
            ),
            uiOutput("rv_cadence_chart"),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small",
              "Two-segment line chart: daily reviews_created (volume) and pass rate ",
              "before and after the cadence reduction on 2026-05-21 (llm#217). ",
              "A vertical marker at the split date separates the before/after periods. ",
              "Green = pass rate (right axis); blue = review volume (left axis). ",
              "Source: roborev_cadence_efficacy.json from roborev_daily_metrics in unified.duckdb. ",
              "Confidential repos excluded via clean_projects(). ",
              "Data refreshed on each local export run and committed to inst/extdata/ for CI serving.")
          )
        ),
        layout_columns(col_widths = c(12),
          card(
            card_header("Severity Threshold History (#146 Q8)"),
            tags$div(class = "p-3", style = "color: #e0e0e0; font-size: 0.9em;",
              tags$p(
                "Step chart of the effective severity threshold level over time, ",
                "overlaid on the daily count of reviews auto-closed by severity. ",
                "Each annotated vertical line marks a threshold change event. ",
                "Threshold levels: ", tags$code("low"), ", ", tags$code("medium"), ", ",
                tags$code("high"), ", ", tags$code("critical"), "."
              )
            ),
            uiOutput("rv_threshold_chart"),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small",
              "Line chart: reviews_autoclosed_severity per day (all repos summed) with ",
              "step-change markers for each threshold change event. ",
              "Threshold level (right axis, step series) sourced from roborev_threshold_changes; ",
              "autoclosed count (left axis) from roborev_daily_metrics. ",
              "Source: roborev_threshold_history.json from unified.duckdb. ",
              "Data refreshed on each local export run and committed to inst/extdata/ for CI serving.")
          )
        )
        ),  # close Operations nav_panel
        nav_panel("Cost",
        layout_columns(col_widths = c(12),
          card(
            card_header("Cost per Finding Resolved (#146 Q1)"),
            tags$div(class = "p-3", style = "color: #e0e0e0; font-size: 0.9em;",
              tags$p(
                "Addressed findings per agent over time. Dollar cost per finding is not yet ",
                "instrumented — ", tags$code("roborev_agent_performance.total_cost_usd"),
                " is NULL as of 2026-05-30. The chart shows addressed finding count ",
                "(", tags$code("close_reason='manual'"), ") as the actionable proxy. ",
                "Addressed = a human manually closed the review after the fixer acted."
              )
            ),
            uiOutput("rv_cpf_chart"),
            tags$table(class = "table table-sm table-dark mt-2 mb-0",
              tags$thead(tags$tr(
                tags$th("Agent"), tags$th("Addressed"), tags$th("Passed"),
                tags$th("Cost/finding (USD)")
              )),
              uiOutput("rv_cpf_table")
            ),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small",
              "Addressed = close_reason='manual' in roborev_review_lifecycle. ",
              "Passed = pass_count in roborev_agent_performance (critic found no issues). ",
              "Cost per finding will show once total_cost_usd instrumentation lands. ",
              "PALETTE: codex=#4ea8de, claude-code=#69d4a0, gemini=#ffd93d. ",
              "Source: roborev_review_lifecycle + roborev_agent_performance in unified.duckdb.")
          )
        ),
        layout_columns(col_widths = c(12),
          card(
            card_header("Cheapest Agent per Resolved Finding (#146 Q11)"),
            tags$div(class = "p-3", style = "color: #e0e0e0; font-size: 0.9em;",
              tags$p(
                "Cleveland dot chart: addressed finding count per agent (x-axis). ",
                "When cost instrumentation is available, the x-axis will show USD per finding. ",
                "Lower x-value = better ROI (more addressed findings per dollar)."
              )
            ),
            uiOutput("rv_cpa_chart"),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small",
              "X-axis: addressed finding count per agent (cost_usd_per_finding when instrumented). ",
              "Source: roborev_cost_per_agent.json from unified.duckdb. ",
              "PALETTE: codex=#4ea8de, claude-code=#69d4a0, gemini=#ffd93d.")
          )
        )
        ),  # close Cost nav_panel
        nav_panel("Detail",
        layout_columns(col_widths = c(12),
          card(
            card_header("Top Commits by Finding Count (#146 Q16)"),
            tags$div(class = "p-3", style = "color: #e0e0e0; font-size: 0.9em;",
              tags$p(
                "Top-10 commits ranked by the number of roborev findings triggered. ",
                "Each row links to the GitHub commit. ",
                "A high finding count on a single commit may indicate a large change set or a ",
                "recurring pattern — cross-reference with the Active Loops panel above."
              )
            ),
            uiOutput("rv_commit_findings_table"),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small",
              "Source: roborev_commit_findings.json from roborev_review_lifecycle in unified.duckdb. ",
              "Commit SHA (7-char prefix) is linked to the GitHub commit page. ",
              "Confidential repos excluded via clean_projects(). ",
              "Data refreshed on each local export run and committed to inst/extdata/ for CI serving.")
          )
        ),
        layout_columns(col_widths = c(12),
          card(
            card_header("Per-Agent Review Noise Rate (#146 Q19)"),
            tags$div(class = "p-3", style = "color: #e0e0e0; font-size: 0.9em;",
              tags$p(
                "Noise rate = percentage of reviews that produced no actionable finding ",
                "(failed without a manual close). ",
                "A lower noise rate means the agent surfaces real findings more reliably. ",
                "Bar chart shows noise rate per agent; summary table shows review volumes."
              )
            ),
            uiOutput("rv_agent_noise_chart"),
            tags$table(class = "table table-sm table-dark mt-2 mb-0",
              tags$thead(tags$tr(
                tags$th("Agent"), tags$th("Reviews"), tags$th("Addressed"),
                tags$th("Passed"), tags$th("Noise rate")
              )),
              uiOutput("rv_agent_noise_table")
            ),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small",
              "Noise rate = 1 - (n_addressed + n_passed) / n_reviews. ",
              "Source: roborev_session_agent_noise.json from roborev_review_lifecycle + roborev_agent_performance in unified.duckdb. ",
              "PALETTE: codex=#4ea8de, claude-code=#69d4a0, gemini=#ffd93d. ",
              "Data refreshed on each local export run and committed to inst/extdata/ for CI serving.")
          )
        ),
        layout_columns(col_widths = c(12),
          card(
            card_header("Session Duration vs Roborev Findings (#146 Q18)"),
            tags$div(class = "p-3", style = "color: #e0e0e0; font-size: 0.9em;",
              tags$p(
                "Do longer sessions produce more roborev findings? ",
                "Each point is one session; x-axis = session duration in hours (h), ",
                "y-axis = number of roborev findings linked to commits made during that session. ",
                "Colour = driving agent (", tags$code("AGENT_PALETTE"), "). ",
                "Session-commit links are computed by the #255 rollup: commits whose date ",
                "falls within [session.started_at, session.ended_at] AND whose project matches ",
                "the session's canonical project."
              )
            ),
            uiOutput("rv_q18_chart"),
            tags$table(class = "table table-sm table-dark mt-2 mb-0",
              tags$thead(tags$tr(
                tags$th("Duration bucket"),
                tags$th("Sessions"),
                tags$th("Median findings")
              )),
              uiOutput("rv_q18_table")
            ),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small",
              "Scatter chart: session duration (h) vs linked roborev findings count per session. ",
              "Only sessions with at least one linked commit AND at least one finding are shown. ",
              "Duration buckets: <1 h, 1–3 h, 3–8 h, >8 h. ",
              "Colour encodes driving agent per AGENT_PALETTE (codex=#4ea8de, claude-code=#69d4a0, gemini=#ffd93d). ",
              "Source: roborev_session_findings.json from sessions.parquet × git_commits.parquet × ",
              "unified.duckdb roborev_review_lifecycle. ",
              "Confidential repos (mycare, crypto, swarms) excluded via clean_projects(). ",
              "Data refreshed on each local export run and committed to inst/extdata/ for CI serving.")
          )
        ),
        layout_columns(col_widths = c(12),
          card(
            card_header("Top Files by Finding Frequency (#146 Q17)"),
            tags$div(class = "p-3", style = "color: #e0e0e0; font-size: 0.9em;",
              tags$p(
                "Which files appear most frequently in roborev findings? ",
                "Top-15 file paths by occurrence count across all public-repo reviews. ",
                "Extracted by parsing ",
                tags$code("**Location**:"), " markup from review output text. ",
                "Severity breakdown (H/M/L) counts how often the file appeared in a ",
                "High-, Medium-, or Low-severity finding. ",
                "Only non-confidential repos included."
              )
            ),
            tags$table(class = "table table-sm table-dark mb-0",
              tags$thead(tags$tr(
                tags$th("File"),
                tags$th(title = "Total occurrences across all reviews", "Count"),
                tags$th(title = "Number of distinct repositories", "Repos"),
                tags$th(title = "Most recent review date containing this file", "Last seen"),
                tags$th(title = "Occurrences in High-severity findings", "H"),
                tags$th(title = "Occurrences in Medium-severity findings", "M"),
                tags$th(title = "Occurrences in Low-severity findings", "L")
              )),
              uiOutput("rv_location_table")
            ),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small",
              "Parsed from roborev_location_findings.json — sourced from ~/.roborev/reviews.db 'output' column. ",
              "Each **Location**: line per review contributes one occurrence. ",
              "Confidential repos (mycare, crypto, swarms) excluded via EXCLUDED_DASHBOARD_PROJECTS filter. ",
              "Absolute and sensitive filesystem paths stripped before aggregation. ",
              "Data refreshed on each local export run and committed to inst/extdata/ for CI serving.")
          )
        ),
        layout_columns(col_widths = c(12),
          card(
            card_header("Time-to-Fix After a Real Finding (#146 Q4)"),
            tags$div(class = "p-3", style = "color: #e0e0e0; font-size: 0.9em;",
              tags$p(
                "Distribution of time from fix commit to review closure, for reviews with an ",
                "explicit fix link (", tags$code("fix_method IN ('manual', 'commit_reference')"), "). ",
                "The fix commit lands first; the reviewer then closes the review — this chart ",
                "shows how quickly that loop closes. n=", textOutput("rv_q4_n_fix", inline = TRUE),
                " fix-linked reviews (sparse — will grow as the ",
                tags$code("roborev #N"), " commit-message convention is adopted, llm#359)."
              )
            ),
            uiOutput("rv_q4_chart"),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small",
              "Horizontal bar chart: count of fix-linked reviews per time-to-close bucket ",
              "(0–2 h / 2–4 h / 4–8 h / 8–16 h / 16+ h). ",
              "fix_method='manual': human explicitly closed after reviewing the fix. ",
              "fix_method='commit_reference': roborev #N notation in commit message. ",
              "n is sparse (21 as of first ETL run 2026-05-31); will grow as llm#359 lands. ",
              "Source: roborev_fix_timing.json from roborev_review_lifecycle in unified.duckdb. ",
              "Data refreshed on each local export run and committed to inst/extdata/ for CI serving.")
          )
        ),
        layout_columns(col_widths = c(12),
          card(
            card_header("Closure Breakdown — False-Positive Rate (#146 Q9)"),
            tags$div(class = "p-3", style = "color: #e0e0e0; font-size: 0.9em;",
              tags$p(
                "Breakdown of all ", textOutput("rv_q9_n_closed", inline = TRUE),
                " closed reviews by closure reason. ",
                "Only ", textOutput("rv_q9_fix_linked_pct", inline = TRUE),
                "% of closures have an explicit fix link — but this is NOT the false-positive rate. ",
                "The dominant bucket is autoclosed-severity (",
                textOutput("rv_q9_autoclose_pct", inline = TRUE),
                "%) — reviews auto-dismissed before human review under the severity threshold ",
                "(llm#224). The genuinely unclassified bucket is 'unknown' (",
                textOutput("rv_q9_n_unknown", inline = TRUE),
                " reviews, ", textOutput("rv_q9_unknown_pct", inline = TRUE),
                "%) — these represent the real investigation backlog."
              )
            ),
            uiOutput("rv_q9_chart"),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small",
              "Horizontal stacked bar: closed reviews split into fix_linked / autoclose / unknown buckets. ",
              "fix_linked = close_reason='manual' OR fix_method='commit_reference'. ",
              "autoclose = fix_method='autoclose_severity' (llm#224 threshold dismissal — not classical false-positives). ",
              "unknown = fix_method='unknown' or NULL after closure (heuristic fall-through — needs investigation). ",
              "Source: roborev_closure_breakdown.json from roborev_review_lifecycle in unified.duckdb. ",
              "Data refreshed on each local export run and committed to inst/extdata/ for CI serving.")
          )
        ),
        layout_columns(col_widths = c(12),
          card(
            card_header("Closing-the-Loop Funnel (#146 Q14)"),
            tags$div(class = "p-3", style = "color: #e0e0e0; font-size: 0.9em;",
              tags$p(
                "Four-step funnel: how many reviews complete the full loop from creation to an ",
                "explicit fix commit? Only ", textOutput("rv_q14_commit_ref", inline = TRUE),
                " reviews have a commit-reference link (the automatic path via ",
                tags$code("roborev #N"), " in commit messages). The manual path accounts for ",
                textOutput("rv_q14_fix_any", inline = TRUE),
                " total fix-linked closures. Funnel narrows sharply at step 3 — growing this ",
                "number is the goal of llm#359."
              )
            ),
            uiOutput("rv_q14_chart"),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small",
              "Bar chart showing 4 funnel steps: (1) all reviews created, (2) closed, ",
              "(3) closed with any explicit fix link (manual + commit_reference), ",
              "(4) closed via commit-reference specifically (roborev #N commit message convention). ",
              "Steps (3)+(4) are sparse today; will grow as llm#359 lands. ",
              "Source: roborev_loop_funnel.json from roborev_review_lifecycle in unified.duckdb. ",
              "Data refreshed on each local export run and committed to inst/extdata/ for CI serving.")
          )
        )
        )  # close Detail nav_panel
        )  # close Reviews navset_tab
      )
    )
  ),

  nav_panel("Reference", value = "Reference",
    navset_tab(
      nav_panel("Calibration", value = "Calibration",
        layout_columns(col_widths = c(6, 6),
          card(card_header("Reliability Diagram"), uiOutput("cal_reliability"),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small", "Predicted confidence vs actual accuracy by bucket. Diagonal = perfect calibration.")),
          card(card_header("Brier Score Trend"), uiOutput("cal_brier"),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small", "Rolling Brier score over time (0 = perfect, 0.25 = uninformative)"))
        ),
        layout_columns(col_widths = c(6, 6),
          card(card_header("Accuracy by Task Type"), uiOutput("cal_by_type"),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small", "Success rate per task category")),
          card(card_header("Accuracy by Project"), uiOutput("cal_by_project"),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small", "Success rate per project · sparse data expected (only resolved predictions logged)"))
        ),
        layout_columns(col_widths = c(12),
          card(card_header("Prediction Log"), DTOutput("cal_log"),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small", "All predictions with stated confidence, outcome, and notes"))
        )
      ),
      nav_panel("Glossary", value = "Glossary", icon = icon("book"),
        # Split into a navset_tab so the four sections no longer stack and
        # overlap (#176 follow-up). The shown.bs.tab handler at L741 already
        # re-renders the mermaid diagrams whenever any tab (including these
        # nested ones) becomes visible — nested tab events bubble up.
        navset_tab(
        nav_panel("Architecture",
        # Architecture card — issue #176 increment 1
        # Approach: Mermaid flowchart TB (top-to-bottom) with clickable nodes (securityLevel loose)
        # + definition list with Tippy hover popups (body-size, >=2 sentences, >=1 link)
        # DL fallback chosen over Tippy-on-SVG-node because SVG node IDs are unstable
        # in WebAssembly/Shinylive context (Mermaid ID generation timing).
        card(card_header("Architecture — Data & Build Flow"),
          tags$div(class = "p-3 arch-mermaid-wrap",
            # Mermaid diagram: refresh-cron -> committed-data -> CI-render -> dashboard/email
            tags$div(class = "mermaid", "
flowchart TB
    classDef default fill:#999999,stroke:#CC0000,stroke-width:1px,color:#000000;
    Logs[\"Claude / Codex / Gemini<br/>usage logs (~/.claude)\"]
    Cron[\"Refresh cron —<br/>refresh_and_preserve.sh<br/>(every 12h)\"]
    Tools[\"ccusage CLI · Gemini API · Codex\"]
    Data[\"Committed data —<br/>inst/extdata/*.json, *.duckdb\"]
    CI[\"CI render —<br/>deploy-dashboard.yaml\"]
    Dash[\"Shinylive dashboard<br/>(this page)\"]
    Email[\"Daily email —<br/>send_daily_email.R<br/>(08:00 UTC)\"]
    Logs --> Cron
    Cron --> Tools
    Tools --> Data
    Data --> CI
    CI --> Dash
    Data --> Email
    click Cron \"https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/blob/main/exec/refresh_and_preserve.sh\" \"View refresh script\" _blank
    click Data \"https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/tree/main/inst/extdata\" \"Browse committed data\" _blank
    click CI \"https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/blob/main/.github/workflows/deploy-dashboard.yaml\" \"View CI workflow\" _blank
    click Dash \"https://johngavin.github.io/llmtelemetry/\" \"Open live dashboard\" _blank
    click Email \"https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/blob/main/inst/scripts/send_daily_email.R\" \"View email script\" _blank
            "),
            tags$p(class = "text-muted small mt-2",
              "Click highlighted nodes to open the relevant source or dataset. ",
              "Hover over component names below for descriptions."),
            # Definition list with Tippy hover popups — hover-popup-standard rule compliance:
            # each term has >=2 sentences, >=1 embedded link, font-size 1rem (via arch theme CSS)
            tags$dl(class = "arch-dl mt-3", style = "color: #e0e0e0; font-size: 0.95em;",
              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>Claude / Codex / Gemini usage logs</b> — Raw telemetry written by Claude Code, ",
                  "OpenAI Codex, and Gemini CLI as JSON/text to <code>~/.claude/</code> and related dirs. ",
                  "These are the primary input to the entire pipeline — without them no cost or session ",
                  "data can be derived. ",
                  "See <a href='https://github.com/ryoppippi/ccusage' target='_blank' rel='noopener'>ccusage</a> ",
                  "for how they are read."
                ),
                "Usage Logs"
              ),
              tags$dd("Raw JSON / text telemetry from Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini CLI (~/.claude). Primary pipeline input."),

              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>Refresh cron — exec/refresh_and_preserve.sh</b> — A shell script scheduled every 12 hours ",
                  "that calls ccusage, the Gemini API, and Codex exporters, then commits the resulting JSON and ",
                  "DuckDB files to the repository. ",
                  "See <a href='https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/blob/main/exec/refresh_and_preserve.sh' ",
                  "target='_blank' rel='noopener'>refresh_and_preserve.sh on GitHub</a>."
                ),
                "Refresh Cron"
              ),
              tags$dd("Shell script (every 12h) that runs data exporters and commits the results."),

              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>ccusage CLI · Gemini API · Codex</b> — The three data-extraction tools: ccusage reads ",
                  "Claude Code session logs, the Gemini API returns token usage, and Codex export parses ",
                  "OpenAI usage. Each produces structured JSON that the cron commits. ",
                  "See <a href='https://github.com/ryoppippi/ccusage' target='_blank' rel='noopener'>ccusage on GitHub</a> ",
                  "for Claude Code extraction details."
                ),
                "Extraction Tools"
              ),
              tags$dd("ccusage CLI (Claude), Gemini API, and Codex exporter — produce the committed JSON data."),

              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>Committed data — inst/extdata/*.json, *.duckdb</b> — The authoritative data store: JSON ",
                  "files (daily, sessions, blocks, calibration, git-recon) and DuckDB files committed to the ",
                  "repository by the refresh cron. These files are the single source of truth for all dashboard ",
                  "tabs. ",
                  "Browse at <a href='https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/tree/main/inst/extdata' ",
                  "target='_blank' rel='noopener'>inst/extdata on GitHub</a>."
                ),
                "Committed Data"
              ),
              tags$dd("JSON + DuckDB files committed to inst/extdata/ — the dashboard's single source of truth."),

              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>CI render — deploy-dashboard.yaml</b> — A GitHub Actions workflow that runs on every push, ",
                  "renders this Quarto Shinylive document, and deploys it to GitHub Pages. The workflow also ",
                  "validates the Mermaid syntax and runs R CMD check. ",
                  "See <a href='https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/blob/main/.github/workflows/deploy-dashboard.yaml' ",
                  "target='_blank' rel='noopener'>deploy-dashboard.yaml on GitHub</a>."
                ),
                "CI Render"
              ),
              tags$dd("GitHub Actions workflow: renders and deploys the dashboard to GitHub Pages on every push."),

              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>Shinylive dashboard (this page)</b> — A Shiny app compiled to WebAssembly and served as a ",
                  "static page via GitHub Pages — no R server required. All data is fetched from the committed ",
                  "JSON files at load time. ",
                  "Live at <a href='https://johngavin.github.io/llmtelemetry/' target='_blank' rel='noopener'>",
                  "johngavin.github.io/llmtelemetry</a>."
                ),
                "Shinylive Dashboard"
              ),
              tags$dd("This page — a static Shinylive/WebAssembly app served from GitHub Pages. No server required."),

              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>Daily email — send_daily_email.R (08:00 UTC)</b> — An R script scheduled at 08:00 UTC that ",
                  "reads the committed data, computes a daily summary (cost, tokens, commits), and sends it as an ",
                  "HTML email. Runs independently of the dashboard render. ",
                  "See <a href='https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/blob/main/inst/scripts/send_daily_email.R' ",
                  "target='_blank' rel='noopener'>send_daily_email.R on GitHub</a>."
                ),
                "Daily Email"
              ),
              tags$dd("R script (08:00 UTC) — reads committed data and sends an HTML daily summary email.")
            )
          ),
          card_footer(class = "text-muted small",
            "Architecture of the llmtelemetry refresh pipeline. ",
            "Click nodes in the diagram to open the relevant GitHub source or dataset.")
        )
        ),  # close Architecture nav_panel
        nav_panel("Workflow",
        # Workflow card — issue #176 increment 2
        # Shows the end-to-end refresh lifecycle: cron triggers -> data pipeline -> CI -> publish
        card(card_header("Workflow — Refresh Lifecycle"),
          tags$div(class = "p-3 arch-mermaid-wrap",
            tags$div(class = "mermaid", "
flowchart TB
    classDef default fill:#999999,stroke:#CC0000,stroke-width:1px,color:#000000;
    classDef cluster fill:#1a1a1a,stroke:#666666,stroke-width:1px,color:#e0e0e0;
    Cron12h[\"Cron (every 12h) —<br/>refresh_and_preserve.sh\"]
    Sanitize[\"Sanitize & redact (privacy)\"]
    Commit[\"git commit + push —<br/>inst/extdata\"]
    CI[\"GitHub Actions —<br/>deploy-dashboard.yaml\"]
    Pages[\"GitHub Pages —<br/>static Shinylive site\"]
    Email[\"send_daily_email.R<br/>(08:00 UTC)\"]
    subgraph fetch [\"Parallel fetch (3 providers)\"]
        direction TB
        FetchCC[\"ccusage fetch —<br/>Claude session logs\"]
        FetchGem[\"Gemini API fetch —<br/>token usage\"]
        FetchCodex[\"Codex export —<br/>OpenAI usage\"]
    end
    Cron12h --> fetch
    fetch --> Sanitize
    Sanitize --> Commit
    Commit --> CI
    CI --> Pages
    Commit --> Email
    click Cron12h \"https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/blob/main/exec/refresh_and_preserve.sh\" \"View refresh script\" _blank
    click CI \"https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/blob/main/.github/workflows/deploy-dashboard.yaml\" \"View CI workflow\" _blank
    click Pages \"https://johngavin.github.io/llmtelemetry/\" \"Open live dashboard\" _blank
    click Email \"https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/blob/main/inst/scripts/send_daily_email.R\" \"View email script\" _blank
            "),
            tags$p(class = "text-muted small mt-2",
              "Click highlighted nodes to open the relevant source or workflow. ",
              "Hover over component names below for descriptions."),
            tags$dl(class = "arch-dl mt-3", style = "color: #e0e0e0; font-size: 0.95em;",
              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>Cron (every 12h) — refresh_and_preserve.sh</b> — A shell script triggered ",
                  "every 12 hours that orchestrates all data fetching, sanitization, and committing steps. ",
                  "It preserves existing data before overwriting, ensuring no telemetry is lost. ",
                  "See <a href='https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/blob/main/exec/refresh_and_preserve.sh' ",
                  "target='_blank' rel='noopener'>refresh_and_preserve.sh on GitHub</a>."
                ),
                "12h Refresh Cron"
              ),
              tags$dd("Shell script run every 12 hours — orchestrates fetch, sanitize, commit, and push."),
              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>ccusage fetch</b> — Calls the ccusage CLI to read Claude Code session JSON logs ",
                  "from <code>~/.claude/</code> and export them as structured JSON (daily, sessions, blocks). ",
                  "ccusage parses raw Claude Code telemetry into cost and token summaries. ",
                  "See <a href='https://github.com/ryoppippi/ccusage' target='_blank' rel='noopener'>",
                  "ccusage on GitHub</a>."
                ),
                "ccusage Fetch"
              ),
              tags$dd("Reads Claude Code logs from ~/.claude and exports daily/sessions/blocks JSON."),
              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>Gemini API fetch</b> — Queries the Google Gemini API to retrieve token usage statistics ",
                  "for Gemini CLI sessions. Results are stored in <code>gemini_usage.duckdb</code>. ",
                  "See <a href='https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs' target='_blank' rel='noopener'>",
                  "Gemini API docs</a>."
                ),
                "Gemini API Fetch"
              ),
              tags$dd("Queries Google Gemini API for token usage; stored in gemini_usage.duckdb."),
              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>Sanitize & redact</b> — Removes personally identifiable information and confidential ",
                  "project paths from the telemetry before committing to the public repository. ",
                  "This step ensures GDPR and privacy compliance for the published data. ",
                  "See the <a href='https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/blob/main/exec/refresh_and_preserve.sh' ",
                  "target='_blank' rel='noopener'>sanitize logic in refresh_and_preserve.sh</a>."
                ),
                "Sanitize & Redact"
              ),
              tags$dd("Removes PII and confidential paths before committing to the public repo."),
              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>GitHub Actions — deploy-dashboard.yaml</b> — Triggered on every push to main, ",
                  "this workflow renders the Quarto Shinylive document and deploys it to GitHub Pages. ",
                  "It also runs R CMD check and validates the output. ",
                  "See <a href='https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/blob/main/.github/workflows/deploy-dashboard.yaml' ",
                  "target='_blank' rel='noopener'>deploy-dashboard.yaml on GitHub</a>."
                ),
                "CI Render & Deploy"
              ),
              tags$dd("GitHub Actions renders Quarto Shinylive and deploys to GitHub Pages on every push."),
              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>send_daily_email.R (08:00 UTC)</b> — An R script scheduled at 08:00 UTC that reads ",
                  "the latest committed data, computes a daily cost/token/commit summary, and sends it as ",
                  "an HTML email to the configured recipients. Runs independently of the dashboard CI. ",
                  "See <a href='https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/blob/main/inst/scripts/send_daily_email.R' ",
                  "target='_blank' rel='noopener'>send_daily_email.R on GitHub</a>."
                ),
                "Daily Email (08:00 UTC)"
              ),
              tags$dd("R script (08:00 UTC) reads committed data and sends an HTML daily summary email.")
            )
          ),
          card_footer(class = "text-muted small",
            "End-to-end refresh lifecycle for llmtelemetry. ",
            "Click nodes in the diagram to open the relevant source or workflow.")
        )
        ),  # close Workflow nav_panel
        nav_panel("Datasets",
        # Datasets card — issue #176 increment 2
        # Shows the committed data files in inst/extdata and what each holds
        card(card_header("Datasets — Committed Data Files"),
          tags$div(class = "p-3 arch-mermaid-wrap",
            tags$div(class = "mermaid", "
flowchart LR
    classDef default fill:#999999,stroke:#CC0000,stroke-width:1px,color:#000000;
    ExtData[\"inst/extdata/ —<br/>committed data root\"]
    CCDaily[\"ccusage_daily_all.json —<br/>daily cost & tokens\"]
    CCSess[\"ccusage_sessions_all.json —<br/>per-session records\"]
    GemDB[\"gemini_usage.duckdb —<br/>Gemini token history\"]
    UsageDB[\"llm_usage_history.duckdb —<br/>unified usage store\"]
    Roborev[\"roborev_summary.json —<br/>code review metrics\"]
    Preds[\"predictions.json —<br/>calibration log\"]
    Cmonitor[\"cmonitor_daily.txt —<br/>system cost monitor\"]
    ExtData --> CCDaily
    ExtData --> CCSess
    ExtData --> GemDB
    ExtData --> UsageDB
    ExtData --> Roborev
    ExtData --> Preds
    ExtData --> Cmonitor
    click ExtData \"https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/tree/main/inst/extdata\" \"Browse all data files\" _blank
    click CCDaily \"https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/tree/main/inst/extdata\" \"Browse extdata\" _blank
    click GemDB \"https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/tree/main/inst/extdata\" \"Browse extdata\" _blank
    click UsageDB \"https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/tree/main/inst/extdata\" \"Browse extdata\" _blank
            "),
            tags$p(class = "text-muted small mt-2",
              "Click highlighted nodes to browse the data files on GitHub. ",
              "Hover over file names below for descriptions."),
            tags$dl(class = "arch-dl mt-3", style = "color: #e0e0e0; font-size: 0.95em;",
              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>ccusage_daily_all.json</b> — Daily aggregated cost and token totals exported by ccusage. ",
                  "Each record covers one calendar day and includes input, output, cache-creation, and ",
                  "cache-read tokens plus total cost in USD. Powers the Cost and Tokens dashboard tabs. ",
                  "Browse at <a href='https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/tree/main/inst/extdata' ",
                  "target='_blank' rel='noopener'>inst/extdata on GitHub</a>."
                ),
                "ccusage_daily_all.json"
              ),
              tags$dd("Daily cost and token aggregates from ccusage. Powers Cost and Tokens tabs."),
              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>ccusage_sessions_all.json</b> — Per-API-conversation records from ccusage. Each record ",
                  "represents a single Claude Code conversation with start time, duration, token counts, ",
                  "project, and model. Powers the Sessions dashboard tab. ",
                  "Browse at <a href='https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/tree/main/inst/extdata' ",
                  "target='_blank' rel='noopener'>inst/extdata on GitHub</a>."
                ),
                "ccusage_sessions_all.json"
              ),
              tags$dd("Per-API-conversation records: start, duration, tokens, model, project."),
              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>gemini_usage.duckdb</b> — DuckDB database holding Gemini CLI token usage history. ",
                  "Queried by duckplyr in the dashboard to show Gemini cost alongside Claude costs. ",
                  "Updated every 12 hours by the refresh cron. ",
                  "See <a href='https://duckdb.org/' target='_blank' rel='noopener'>DuckDB</a> for the format."
                ),
                "gemini_usage.duckdb"
              ),
              tags$dd("DuckDB file: Gemini CLI token usage history, updated every 12 hours."),
              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>llm_usage_history.duckdb</b> — Unified DuckDB store combining Claude, Gemini, and Codex ",
                  "usage records into a single queryable database. Supports cross-provider cost comparison ",
                  "in the dashboard. ",
                  "See <a href='https://duckdb.org/' target='_blank' rel='noopener'>DuckDB</a> for the format."
                ),
                "llm_usage_history.duckdb"
              ),
              tags$dd("Unified DuckDB: all providers (Claude, Gemini, Codex) in one queryable store."),
              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>roborev_summary.json</b> — JSON export of code-review metrics from the roborev tool. ",
                  "Includes review counts, open/closed findings, and per-project severity breakdown. ",
                  "Powers the Git/Commits dashboard tab. ",
                  "See <a href='https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry' target='_blank' rel='noopener'>",
                  "llmtelemetry on GitHub</a>."
                ),
                "roborev_summary.json"
              ),
              tags$dd("Code-review metrics from roborev: counts, findings, severity by project."),
              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>predictions.json</b> — The calibration log: each record is a stated prediction with ",
                  "confidence, resolution date, actual outcome, and notes. Used to compute Brier score and ",
                  "the reliability diagram in the Calibration tab. ",
                  "See <a href='https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Calibration_(statistics)' ",
                  "target='_blank' rel='noopener'>statistical calibration on Wikipedia</a>."
                ),
                "predictions.json"
              ),
              tags$dd("Calibration log: predictions with confidence, outcome, and notes for Brier score."),
              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>cmonitor_daily.txt</b> — Daily system-level cost-monitor output (plain text). Captures ",
                  "total API spend and burn rate at a system level, independent of ccusage. Provides a ",
                  "cross-check against the ccusage daily totals. ",
                  "See the <a href='https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/blob/main/exec/refresh_and_preserve.sh' ",
                  "target='_blank' rel='noopener'>refresh script</a> for how it is generated."
                ),
                "cmonitor_daily.txt"
              ),
              tags$dd("System-level daily cost monitor output — cross-checks ccusage totals.")
            )
          ),
          card_footer(class = "text-muted small",
            "All data files committed to inst/extdata/ on every 12-hour refresh. ",
            "Click nodes to browse on GitHub.")
        )
        ),  # close Datasets nav_panel
        nav_panel("Metric Definitions",
        card(card_header("Metric Definitions"),
          tags$div(class = "p-3", style = "color: #e0e0e0; font-size: 0.9em;",
            navset_tab(
              nav_panel("Cost",
                tags$dl(class = "mt-2",
                  tags$dt("Total Cost ($)"), tags$dd("Sum of API charges for input, output, and cache tokens. ",
                    tags$a(href = "https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/about-claude/models/overview#api-models", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer", style = "color:#7cb5ec;", "[Anthropic pricing]")),
                  tags$dt("Cost per MTok"), tags$dd("Cost per million tokens — efficiency metric comparing model tiers."),
                  tags$dt("Cumulative Cost"), tags$dd("Running total of spend over time. Useful for burn-rate forecasting."),
                  tags$dt("Est. Cost by Project"), tags$dd("⚠ Approximate: daily cost × (project session time / total session time). No per-project billing from API.")
                )
              ),
              nav_panel("Tokens",
                tags$dl(class = "mt-2",
                  tags$dt("Input Tokens"), tags$dd("Tokens sent to the model (prompts, context)."),
                  tags$dt("Output Tokens"), tags$dd("Tokens generated by the model (responses)."),
                  tags$dt("Cache Creation"), tags$dd("Tokens written to prompt cache (first use). Billed at 25% of base input rate. ",
                    tags$a(href = "https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/prompt-caching", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer", style = "color:#7cb5ec;", "[Anthropic caching docs]")),
                  tags$dt("Cache Read"), tags$dd("Tokens retrieved from cache (subsequent uses). Billed at 10% of base input rate — significant cost saving for repeated context. ",
                    tags$a(href = "https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/prompt-caching", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer", style = "color:#7cb5ec;", "[Anthropic caching docs]"))
                )
              ),
              nav_panel("Sessions",
                tags$dl(class = "mt-2",
                  tags$dt("API Conversation"), tags$dd("Single Claude Code conversation. Short durations (0-5 min) are normal — each context compaction or restart creates a new conversation. ",
                    tags$a(href = "https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/overview", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer", style = "color:#7cb5ec;", "[Claude Code docs]")),
                  tags$dt("Duration (min)"), tags$dd("Time from conversation start to end. NOT user work time — excludes idle periods between messages."),
                  tags$dt("Block"), tags$dd("Contiguous coding period from ccusage; gaps >30 min create new blocks. More meaningful than API conversations as a work-session metric. ",
                    tags$a(href = "https://github.com/ryoppippi/ccusage", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer", style = "color:#7cb5ec;", "[ccusage]"))
                )
              ),
              nav_panel("Git",
                tags$dl(class = "mt-2",
                  tags$dt("Bus Factor"), tags$dd("Risk metric: if 1 person has >60% of commits, project is at risk if they leave. Low bus factor = knowledge concentration. ",
                    tags$a(href = "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bus_factor", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer", style = "color:#7cb5ec;", "[Wikipedia]")),
                  tags$dt("Velocity Trend"), tags$dd("Commits per month — indicates project momentum. Declining velocity may signal reduced activity or scope completion."),
                  tags$dt("Commit Timing"), tags$dd("Heatmap of when commits happen by hour and day. Shows work patterns and potential burnout signals."),
                  tags$dt("File Growth"), tags$dd("6-month rolling aggregate of files added vs deleted. Net positive = codebase growing."),
                  tags$dt("Churn Hotspots"), tags$dd("Files modified most often. High churn = refactoring candidates or unstable design. ",
                    tags$a(href = "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Software_entropy", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer", style = "color:#7cb5ec;", "[Software entropy]")),
                  tags$dt("Bug Hotspots"), tags$dd("Files frequently touched by fix/bug commits. May indicate code quality issues."),
                  tags$dt("TODO/FIXME Debt"), tags$dd("Count of TODO, FIXME, HACK markers. Technical debt that should be tracked and resolved. ",
                    tags$a(href = "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Technical_debt", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer", style = "color:#7cb5ec;", "[Technical debt]")),
                  tags$dt("Crisis Commits"), tags$dd("Reverts, hotfixes, emergency commits. High count = unstable releases or reactive development culture.")
                )
              ),
              nav_panel("Calibration",
                tags$dl(class = "mt-2",
                  tags$dt("Calibration"), tags$dd("How well predicted confidence matches actual outcomes. 80% confident predictions should succeed ~80% of the time. ",
                    tags$a(href = "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Calibration_(statistics)", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer", style = "color:#7cb5ec;", "[Wikipedia]")),
                  tags$dt("Brier Score"), tags$dd("Mean squared error of probabilistic predictions. 0 = perfect, 0.25 = no better than guessing 50%. Lower is better. ",
                    tags$a(href = "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brier_score", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer", style = "color:#7cb5ec;", "[Wikipedia]")),
                  tags$dt("Reliability Diagram"), tags$dd("Plot of predicted confidence vs actual success rate. Points on diagonal = perfectly calibrated. Above = overconfident. Below = underconfident."),
                  tags$dt("Overconfidence"), tags$dd("Predictions above the reliability diagram diagonal — stated confidence exceeds actual accuracy."),
                  tags$dt("Underconfidence"), tags$dd("Predictions below the diagonal — actual accuracy exceeds stated confidence (rare but possible).")
                )
              ),
              nav_panel("Tools",
                tags$dl(class = "mt-2",
                  tags$dt("Shinylive"), tags$dd("R Shiny apps running entirely in the browser via WebAssembly — no server required. ",
                    tags$a(href = "https://posit-dev.github.io/r-shinylive/", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer", style = "color:#7cb5ec;", "[posit-dev.github.io]")),
                  tags$dt("WebR / WebAssembly"), tags$dd("R compiled to WebAssembly, running natively in the browser. Powers the Shinylive embedding in this dashboard. ",
                    tags$a(href = "https://docs.r-wasm.org/webr/latest/", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer", style = "color:#7cb5ec;", "[docs.r-wasm.org]")),
                  tags$dt("DuckDB-WASM"), tags$dd("DuckDB compiled to WebAssembly — SQL analytics in the browser, no server. Used here for Parquet queries. ",
                    tags$a(href = "https://duckdb.org/docs/clients/wasm/overview.html", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer", style = "color:#7cb5ec;", "[DuckDB WASM docs]")),
                  tags$dt("duckplyr"), tags$dd("Tidyverse-syntax DuckDB queries in R — replaces raw SQL strings. ",
                    tags$a(href = "https://duckplyr.tidyverse.org/", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer", style = "color:#7cb5ec;", "[duckplyr.tidyverse.org]")),
                  tags$dt("ECharts"), tags$dd("Apache ECharts — the JavaScript charting library powering all interactive charts. ",
                    tags$a(href = "https://echarts.apache.org/en/index.html", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer", style = "color:#7cb5ec;", "[echarts.apache.org]"))
                )
              )
            )
          ),
          card_footer(class = "text-muted small",
            "Reference definitions for interpreting dashboard metrics. Links open in a new tab. Hover over charts for tooltips.")
        )
        )  # close Metric Definitions nav_panel
        )  # close navset_tab
      ),
      nav_panel("Config Health", value = "Config Health", icon = icon("gauge"),
        layout_columns(col_widths = c(12),
          card(
            card_header(
              "Config Item Staleness",
              toolbar(
                align = "right",
                toolbar_input_select(
                  "cfg_type_filter",
                  label = NULL,
                  choices = c("All types" = "all", "skill" = "skill",
                              "rule" = "rule", "agent" = "agent",
                              "memory" = "memory", "hook" = "hook",
                              "script" = "script"),
                  selected = "all"
                )
              )
            ),
            uiOutput("cfg_staleness_summary"),
            DTOutput("cfg_staleness_table"),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small",
              paste0(
                "Data from ~/.claude/logs/unified.duckdb via skill_usage_etl.R. ",
                "Staleness: never_used = no recorded invocation; ",
                "stale_90d/30d/7d = days since last session use; ",
                "active = used within the last 7 days; mandatory = always-loaded rules."
              )
            )
          )
        )
      )
    )
  ),

  footer = tags$footer(
    class = "text-muted small text-center py-2",
    style = "background-color: #1a1a1a;",
    tags$a(href = "https://github.com/johngavin/llmtelemetry", style = "color: #7cb5ec;", "GitHub"),
    " | Data: ccusage, Gemini, Codex, cmonitor, unified.duckdb, git-recon, predictions",
    " | Built: ", format(Sys.Date(), "%Y-%m-%d")
  ),
  id = "tabs"
)

# --- Server -------------------------------------------------------------------
server <- function(input, output, session) {
  cutoff <- reactive(date_max - input$horizon)

  cc_d <- reactive({
    if (!has_rows(cc_daily)) return(data.frame())
    cc_daily[cc_daily$date >= cutoff(), ]
  })
  # cb_d: CodexBar primary cost source (#281 Phase 3a).
  # cb_per_day schema: {date, model, cost_usd, total_tokens}
  # One row per (date, model); callers aggregate over models as needed.
  cb_d <- reactive({
    if (!has_rows(cb_per_day)) return(data.frame())
    cb_per_day[cb_per_day$date >= cutoff(), ]
  })
  gem_d <- reactive({
    if (!has_rows(gem_daily)) return(data.frame())
    gem_daily[gem_daily$date >= cutoff(), ]
  })
  codex_d <- reactive({
    if (!has_rows(codex_daily)) return(data.frame())
    codex_daily[codex_daily$date >= cutoff(), ]
  })
  # codex_d_proj: when specific projects are selected, filter by canonical_project.
  # When "All projects" is selected (resolve_sel returns NULL), returns codex_d() unchanged.
  codex_d_proj <- reactive({
    sel <- resolve_sel(input$selected_projects)
    d   <- codex_d()
    if (is.null(sel) || nrow(d) == 0) return(d)
    if (!"canonical_project" %in% names(d)) return(d)
    d[d$canonical_project %in% sel, , drop = FALSE]
  })
  # cc_d_proj: when specific projects are selected, weight tokens from tok_proj
  # (tokens_by_project.json). When "All projects" is selected, resolve_sel()
  # returns NULL so is.null(sel) is TRUE, and we fall back to the authoritative
  # cc_d() (legacy_ccusage_daily.json) — no dates or live projects dropped.
  # When "None" is selected, resolve_sel() returns character(0) and
  # d[d$project %in% sel, ] returns 0 rows (correct empty result).
  cc_d_proj <- reactive({
    sel <- resolve_sel(input$selected_projects)
    if (is.null(sel) || !has_rows(tok_proj)) return(cc_d())
    d <- tok_proj
    if (!is.null(cutoff())) d <- d[as.Date(d$date) >= cutoff(), ]
    d <- d[d$project %in% sel, , drop = FALSE]
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(data.frame())
    # Rename to match cc_daily column names so token charts need no changes
    names(d)[names(d) == "est_total_tokens"]   <- "totalTokens"
    names(d)[names(d) == "est_input_tokens"]   <- "inputTokens"
    names(d)[names(d) == "est_output_tokens"]  <- "outputTokens"
    names(d)[names(d) == "est_cache_creation"] <- "cacheCreation"
    names(d)[names(d) == "est_cache_read"]     <- "cacheRead"
    # Aggregate across selected projects -> one row per date
    aggregate(
      cbind(totalTokens, inputTokens, outputTokens, cacheCreation, cacheRead) ~ date,
      data = d, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE
    )
  })

  # --- Phase B: one-shot sidebar population from projects_master.json ----------
  # CRITICAL: choices are populated ONCE on session start by virtue of being
  # called directly in the server body (no reactive wrapper). The original
  # observe(..., once = TRUE) was invalid — `once` is an observeEvent() arg,
  # not an observe() arg, and broke the entire server function.
  # This fixes W4 (stickiness: selector resetting on time-horizon change).
  local({
    master_choices <- c(
      "All projects" = "__ALL__",
      "None"         = "__NONE__",
      setNames(all_projects, all_projects)
    )
    updateSelectInput(session, "selected_projects",
                      choices  = master_choices,
                      selected = "__ALL__")
  })

  # --- Project filter buttons -------------------------------------------------
  observeEvent(input$sel_all, {
    updateSelectInput(session, "selected_projects", selected = "__ALL__")
  })
  observeEvent(input$sel_none, {
    updateSelectInput(session, "selected_projects", selected = "__NONE__")
  })
  observeEvent(input$sel_invert, {
    current <- input$selected_projects
    # Handle sentinel states first — invert of ALL is NONE and vice versa
    if (identical(current, "__ALL__")) {
      updateSelectInput(session, "selected_projects", selected = "__NONE__")
      return()
    }
    if (identical(current, "__NONE__")) {
      updateSelectInput(session, "selected_projects", selected = "__ALL__")
      return()
    }
    # Real project list: invert selection relative to all_projects
    inverted <- setdiff(all_projects, current)
    new_sel <- if (length(inverted) == 0) "__NONE__"
               else if (length(inverted) == length(all_projects)) "__ALL__"
               else inverted
    updateSelectInput(session, "selected_projects", selected = new_sel)
  })

  # resolve_sel: translate selectInput sentinels to a project vector.
  #   NULL or "__ALL__" -> return NULL  (All projects: callers skip the IN filter,
  #                                       so live projects absent from master are included)
  #   "__NONE__"        -> return character(0)  (None: callers filter to zero rows)
  #   character vector  -> return as-is  (specific selection)
  resolve_sel <- function(sel) {
    if (is.null(sel) || "__ALL__" %in% sel) return(NULL)
    if ("__NONE__" %in% sel) return(character(0))
    sel
  }

  # --- Overview ---------------------------------------------------------------
  # #281 Phase 3b: CodexBar primary for Overview KPIs.
  # cb_d() covers Claude (claude-*) AND OpenAI (gpt-*) on a single per-date×model
  # rollup. ccusage and codex_daily are both subsets of CodexBar's coverage,
  # so summing all three double-counts. Gemini is NOT in CodexBar (no upstream
  # provider integration yet), so it stays additive.
  # If cb_d() is empty for the window (e.g. cutoff predates 2026-05-04), fall
  # back to (ccusage + codex est) so the dashboard still renders during the
  # transition period.
  overview_cost_primary <- reactive({
    if (nrow(cb_d()) > 0 && "cost_usd" %in% names(cb_d())) {
      list(value = sum(cb_d()$cost_usd, na.rm = TRUE), source = "codexbar")
    } else {
      cc <- if (nrow(cc_d()) > 0) sum(cc_d()$totalCost, na.rm = TRUE) else 0
      cx <- if (nrow(codex_d()) > 0 && "est_cost_usd" %in% names(codex_d()))
              sum(codex_d()$est_cost_usd, na.rm = TRUE) else 0
      list(value = cc + cx, source = "ccusage+codex (fallback)")
    }
  })
  overview_tokens_primary <- reactive({
    if (nrow(cb_d()) > 0 && "total_tokens" %in% names(cb_d())) {
      sum(cb_d()$total_tokens, na.rm = TRUE)
    } else {
      cc <- if (nrow(cc_d()) > 0) sum(cc_d()$totalTokens, na.rm = TRUE) else 0
      cx <- if (nrow(codex_d()) > 0 && "total_tokens" %in% names(codex_d()))
              sum(codex_d()$total_tokens, na.rm = TRUE) else 0
      cc + cx
    }
  })
  output$vb_cost <- renderText({
    primary <- overview_cost_primary()$value
    gm <- if (nrow(gem_d()) > 0) sum(gem_d()$total_cost, na.rm = TRUE) else 0
    sprintf("$%s", format(round(primary + gm), big.mark = ","))
  })
  output$vb_tokens <- renderText({
    primary <- overview_tokens_primary()
    gm <- if (nrow(gem_d()) > 0) sum(gem_d()$total_tokens, na.rm = TRUE) else 0
    total <- primary + gm
    if (total >= 1e9) sprintf("%.1fB", total / 1e9)
    else if (total >= 1e6) sprintf("%.1fM", total / 1e6)
    else format(total, big.mark = ",")
  })
  output$vb_days <- renderText({
    # Use CodexBar dates when available; fall back to ccusage + codex.
    primary_dates <- if (nrow(cb_d()) > 0) unique(cb_d()$date) else c(
      if (nrow(cc_d()) > 0) unique(cc_d()$date),
      if (nrow(codex_d()) > 0) unique(codex_d()$date)
    )
    dates <- c(primary_dates,
               if (nrow(gem_d()) > 0) unique(gem_d()$date))
    as.character(length(unique(dates)))
  })
  # Cross-check Δ%: ccusage (Claude only) vs CodexBar (Claude+OpenAI) for the
  # same window. Lands #281 Phase 1's "cross-check" pattern on the Overview KPI
  # block. Reads NULL when CodexBar isn't the active primary (fallback path).
  output$vb_cc_delta <- renderText({
    if (overview_cost_primary()$source != "codexbar") return("— (CodexBar fallback)")
    cb <- if (nrow(cb_d()) > 0 && "cost_usd" %in% names(cb_d())) {
      # ccusage covers Claude only — compare to CodexBar Claude rows only.
      sum(cb_d()$cost_usd[grepl("^claude", cb_d()$model)], na.rm = TRUE)
    } else 0
    cc <- if (nrow(cc_d()) > 0) sum(cc_d()$totalCost, na.rm = TRUE) else 0
    if (cb <= 0) return("— (no CodexBar Claude rows)")
    delta_pct <- 100 * (cc - cb) / cb
    sprintf("%+.1f%% (ccusage vs CodexBar Claude)", delta_pct)
  })
  output$vb_sessions <- renderText({
    n <- 0L
    if (has_rows(u_sessions)) {
      n <- nrow(u_sessions[u_sessions$date >= cutoff(), ])
    }
    if (has_rows(gem_sess)) {
      n <- n + nrow(gem_sess[gem_sess$date >= cutoff(), ])
    }
    # Include codex sessions (per-thread, not per-day-row) with the same cutoff
    if (has_rows(codex_sess) && "date" %in% names(codex_sess)) {
      n <- n + nrow(codex_sess[codex_sess$date >= cutoff(), ])
    }
    as.character(n)
  })

  output$overview_table <- renderDT({
    rows <- list()
    if (nrow(cc_d()) > 0) {
      d <- cc_d()
      rows[[1]] <- data.frame(
        Source = "Claude (ccusage)",
        Cost = sprintf("$%.2f", sum(d$totalCost, na.rm = TRUE)),
        `$/Day` = sprintf("$%.2f", sum(d$totalCost, na.rm = TRUE) / max(length(unique(d$date)), 1)),
        Tokens = format(sum(d$totalTokens, na.rm = TRUE), big.mark = ","),
        Days = length(unique(d$date)),
        Sessions = if (has_rows(u_sessions)) nrow(u_sessions[u_sessions$date >= cutoff(), ]) else 0L,
        `Date Range` = paste(min(d$date), "to", max(d$date)),
        check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
    }
    if (nrow(gem_d()) > 0) {
      d <- gem_d()
      rows[[length(rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(
        Source = "Gemini",
        Cost = sprintf("$%.2f", sum(d$total_cost, na.rm = TRUE)),
        `$/Day` = sprintf("$%.2f", sum(d$total_cost, na.rm = TRUE) / max(length(unique(d$date)), 1)),
        Tokens = format(sum(d$total_tokens, na.rm = TRUE), big.mark = ","),
        Days = length(unique(d$date)),
        Sessions = if (has_rows(gem_sess)) nrow(gem_sess[gem_sess$date >= cutoff(), ]) else 0L,
        `Date Range` = paste(min(d$date), "to", max(d$date)),
        check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
    }
    if (nrow(codex_d()) > 0 && "est_cost_usd" %in% names(codex_d())) {
      d <- codex_d()
      rows[[length(rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(
        Source = "Codex (est.)",
        Cost = sprintf("$%.2f", sum(d$est_cost_usd, na.rm = TRUE)),
        `$/Day` = sprintf("$%.2f", sum(d$est_cost_usd, na.rm = TRUE) / max(length(unique(d$date)), 1)),
        Tokens = format(sum(d$total_tokens, na.rm = TRUE), big.mark = ","),
        Days = length(unique(d$date)),
        Sessions = if (has_rows(codex_sess) && "date" %in% names(codex_sess))
                     nrow(codex_sess[codex_sess$date >= cutoff(), ]) else 0L,
        `Date Range` = paste(min(d$date), "to", max(d$date)),
        check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
    }
    if (!is.null(cmon) && !is.null(cmon$total_cost)) {
      rows[[length(rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(
        Source = "cmonitor",
        Cost = sprintf("$%.2f", cmon$total_cost),
        `$/Day` = "-",
        Tokens = format(cmon$total_tokens, big.mark = ","),
        Days = NA_integer_,
        Sessions = NA_integer_,
        `Date Range` = if (!is.null(cmon$date_range)) cmon$date_range else "-",
        check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
    }
    tbl <- if (length(rows) > 0) do.call(rbind, rows) else data.frame()
    datatable(tbl, options = list(dom = "t", paging = FALSE, searching = FALSE,
        scrollX = TRUE, autoWidth = FALSE), rownames = FALSE)
  })

  output$overview_cost_ts <- renderUI({
    has_cc  <- nrow(cc_d()) > 0
    has_gem <- nrow(gem_d()) > 0
    has_cx  <- nrow(codex_d()) > 0 && "est_cost_usd" %in% names(codex_d())
    if (!has_cc && !has_gem && !has_cx) return(ec_empty(h = "350px"))

    # Aggregate each source by date
    cc_agg <- gem_agg <- cx_agg <- NULL
    if (has_cc) {
      cc_agg <- aggregate(totalCost ~ date, data = cc_d(), FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
      names(cc_agg)[2] <- "claude"
    }
    if (has_gem) {
      gem_agg <- aggregate(total_cost ~ date, data = gem_d(), FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
      names(gem_agg)[2] <- "gemini"
    }
    if (has_cx) {
      cx_agg <- aggregate(est_cost_usd ~ date, data = codex_d(), FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
      names(cx_agg)[2] <- "codex"
    }

    # Merge all sources on date (full outer join chain)
    d <- data.frame(date = sort(unique(c(
      if (!is.null(cc_agg)) cc_agg$date,
      if (!is.null(gem_agg)) gem_agg$date,
      if (!is.null(cx_agg)) cx_agg$date
    ))))
    if (!is.null(cc_agg))  d <- merge(d, cc_agg,  by = "date", all.x = TRUE)
    if (!is.null(gem_agg)) d <- merge(d, gem_agg, by = "date", all.x = TRUE)
    if (!is.null(cx_agg))  d <- merge(d, cx_agg,  by = "date", all.x = TRUE)
    for (col in c("claude", "gemini", "codex")) {
      if (col %in% names(d)) d[[col]][is.na(d[[col]])] <- 0
    }
    d <- d[order(d$date), ]

    series <- list()
    if ("claude" %in% names(d))
      series[[length(series) + 1]] <- list(name = "Claude", type = "line",
        data = I(round(d$claude, 2)), itemStyle = list(color = pal$claude))
    if ("gemini" %in% names(d))
      series[[length(series) + 1]] <- list(name = "Gemini", type = "line",
        data = I(round(d$gemini, 2)), itemStyle = list(color = pal$gemini))
    if ("codex" %in% names(d))
      series[[length(series) + 1]] <- list(name = "Codex", type = "line",
        data = I(round(d$codex, 2)), itemStyle = list(color = pal$codex))
    ec_line(d$date, series, "350px", y_name = "Cost ($)")
  })

  # --- Cost Trends ------------------------------------------------------------
  output$cost_by_project <- renderUI({
    d <- cost_proj
