    if (!is.data.frame(d) || nrow(d) == 0) return(ec_empty("No project cost estimates available"))
    # Apply time filter
    if (!is.null(cutoff())) d <- d[d$date >= cutoff(), ]
    # Apply project filter from canonical master list
    sel <- resolve_sel(input$selected_projects)
    if (!is.null(sel)) {
      d <- d[d$project %in% sel, ]
    }
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(ec_empty("No data for selected filters"))

    all_d <- sort(unique(d$date))
    projects <- unique(d$project)
    series <- lapply(projects, function(p) {
      pd <- d[d$project == p, ]
      vals <- rep(0, length(all_d))
      vals[match(pd$date, all_d)] <- pd$est_cost
      list(name = p, type = "line", data = I(round(vals, 2)))
    })
    ec_line(all_d, series, y_name = "Estimated Cost ($)",
            aria_label = "Estimated daily cost by project (session-weighted)")
  })

  output$cost_cumulative <- renderUI({
    # Primary: CodexBar daily total (cb_per_day.json) (#281 Phase 3a)
    # Falls back to cost_proj session-weighted estimate when CodexBar absent
    cb <- cb_d()
    use_cb <- has_rows(cb) && "cost_usd" %in% names(cb)
    if (use_cb) {
      daily <- aggregate(cost_usd ~ date, data = cb, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
      names(daily)[2] <- "cost"
      daily <- daily[order(daily$date), ]
      series <- list(list(
        name = "Total (CodexBar)", type = "line",
        data = I(round(cumsum(daily$cost), 2)),
        itemStyle = list(color = pal$claude),
        areaStyle = list(opacity = 0.1)
      ))
      ec_line(daily$date, series, y_name = "Cumulative Cost ($)",
              aria_label = "Cumulative cost — primary source: CodexBar codexbar_cost_per_day.json")
    } else {
      # Fallback: session-weighted project estimate
      d <- cost_proj
      if (!is.data.frame(d) || nrow(d) == 0)
        return(ec_empty("No cumulative cost data available"))
      if (!is.null(cutoff())) d <- d[d$date >= cutoff(), ]
      sel <- resolve_sel(input$selected_projects)
      if (!is.null(sel)) d <- d[d$project %in% sel, ]
      if (nrow(d) == 0) return(ec_empty("No data for selected projects / period"))
      daily <- aggregate(est_cost ~ date, data = d, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
      daily <- daily[order(daily$date), ]
      series <- list(list(
        name = "Total (est.)", type = "line",
        data = I(round(cumsum(daily$est_cost), 2)),
        itemStyle = list(color = pal$claude),
        areaStyle = list(opacity = 0.1)
      ))
      ec_line(daily$date, series, y_name = "Cumulative Cost ($)",
              aria_label = "Cumulative cost for selected projects (session-weighted estimate)")
    }
  })

  output$cost_weekly <- renderUI({
    # W5 from #72: Weekly Cost by Source — Claude (CodexBar primary), Gemini, Codex
    # (#281 Phase 3a): Claude series now uses cb_d() (CodexBar primary).
    # Falls back to cc_d() (legacy ccusage) when CodexBar data absent.
    # SOURCE_PALETTE is used for all three series (narrative-colour-persistence rule).
    rows <- list()
    # Claude: CodexBar primary (cb_per_day.json), fallback to ccusage
    if (has_rows(cb_d()) && "cost_usd" %in% names(cb_d())) {
      d <- cb_d()
      d$week <- floor_date(d$date, "week")
      a <- aggregate(cost_usd ~ week, data = d, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
      names(a)[2] <- "totalCost"
      a$source <- "Claude"
      rows[[1]] <- a
    } else if (nrow(cc_d()) > 0 && "totalCost" %in% names(cc_d())) {
      d <- cc_d()
      d$week <- floor_date(d$date, "week")
      a <- aggregate(totalCost ~ week, data = d, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
      a$source <- "Claude"
      rows[[1]] <- a
    }
    if (nrow(gem_d()) > 0 && "total_cost" %in% names(gem_d())) {
      d <- gem_d()
      d$week <- floor_date(d$date, "week")
      a <- aggregate(total_cost ~ week, data = d, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
      names(a)[2] <- "totalCost"
      a$source <- "Gemini"
      rows[[length(rows) + 1]] <- a
    }
    if (nrow(codex_d_proj()) > 0 && "est_cost_usd" %in% names(codex_d_proj())) {
      d <- codex_d_proj()
      d$week <- floor_date(d$date, "week")
      a <- aggregate(est_cost_usd ~ week, data = d, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
      names(a)[2] <- "totalCost"
      a$source <- "Codex"
      rows[[length(rows) + 1]] <- a
    }
    if (length(rows) == 0) return(ec_empty())
    d <- do.call(rbind, rows)
    all_weeks <- sort(unique(d$week))
    sources <- unique(d$source)
    src_color <- c(Claude = SOURCE_PALETTE[["claude"]],
                   Gemini = SOURCE_PALETTE[["gemini"]],
                   Codex  = SOURCE_PALETTE[["codex"]])
    series <- lapply(sources, function(s) {
      sd <- d[d$source == s, ]
      vals <- rep(0, length(all_weeks))
      vals[match(sd$week, all_weeks)] <- sd$totalCost
      list(name = s, type = "scatter", symbol = "circle", symbolSize = 10,
           data = I(round(vals, 2)),
           itemStyle = list(color = unname(src_color[s])))
    })
    ec_bar(all_weeks, series)
  })

  output$cost_by_model <- renderUI({
    # (#281 Phase 3a): primary source is CodexBar cb_per_day which already has
    # per-model rows; fall back to ccusage modelsUsed expansion when absent.
    cb <- cb_d()
    if (has_rows(cb) && "cost_usd" %in% names(cb) && "model" %in% names(cb)) {
      # CodexBar: each row is already one (date, model) pair
      cb$week <- floor_date(cb$date, "week")
      agg <- aggregate(cost_usd ~ week + model, data = cb, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
      names(agg)[3] <- "totalCost"
      all_weeks <- sort(unique(agg$week))
      models <- unique(agg$model)
      # Use PALETTE if model matches a known key, otherwise hash to a colour
      pal_ext <- c(pal$claude, pal$gemini, pal$codex, pal$purple, pal$orange, pal$accent)
      series <- lapply(seq_along(models), function(i) {
        m <- models[[i]]
        md <- agg[agg$model == m, ]
        vals <- rep(0, length(all_weeks))
        vals[match(md$week, all_weeks)] <- md$totalCost
        list(name = clean_model(m), type = "line", data = I(round(vals, 2)),
             itemStyle = list(color = pal_ext[((i - 1) %% length(pal_ext)) + 1]))
      })
      ec_line(all_weeks, series)
    } else {
      # Fallback: ccusage modelsUsed expansion
      d <- cc_d()
      if (nrow(d) == 0 || is.null(d$modelsUsed)) return(ec_empty())
      models_split <- strsplit(as.character(d$modelsUsed), ",\\s*")
      n_each <- lengths(models_split)
      d_exp <- d[rep(seq_len(nrow(d)), n_each), ]
      d_exp$model <- clean_model(unlist(models_split))
      d_exp <- d_exp[d_exp$model != "", ]
      d_exp$week <- floor_date(d_exp$date, "week")
      agg <- aggregate(totalCost ~ week + model, data = d_exp, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
      all_weeks <- sort(unique(agg$week))
      models <- unique(agg$model)
      series <- lapply(models, function(m) {
        md <- agg[agg$model == m, ]
        vals <- rep(0, length(all_weeks))
        vals[match(md$week, all_weeks)] <- md$totalCost
        list(name = m, type = "line", data = I(round(vals, 2)))
      })
      ec_line(all_weeks, series)
    }
  })

  # --- Token Analysis ---------------------------------------------------------
  output$token_daily <- renderUI({
    has_cc <- nrow(cc_d_proj()) > 0
    has_gem <- nrow(gem_d()) > 0
    if (!has_cc && !has_gem) return(ec_empty(h = "350px"))

    cc_tok <- gem_tok <- NULL
    if (has_cc) {
      cc_tok <- aggregate(totalTokens ~ date, data = cc_d_proj(), FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
      names(cc_tok)[2] <- "claude"
    }
    if (has_gem) {
      gem_tok <- aggregate(total_tokens ~ date, data = gem_d(), FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
      names(gem_tok)[2] <- "gemini"
    }

    if (has_cc && has_gem) {
      d <- merge(cc_tok, gem_tok, by = "date", all = TRUE)
      d$claude[is.na(d$claude)] <- 0
      d$gemini[is.na(d$gemini)] <- 0
      d <- d[order(d$date), ]
      series <- list(
        list(name = "Claude", type = "line", data = I(d$claude),
             itemStyle = list(color = pal$claude)),
        list(name = "Gemini", type = "line", data = I(d$gemini),
             itemStyle = list(color = pal$gemini)))
      ec_line(d$date, series, "350px", y_name = "Tokens (M)")
    } else if (has_cc) {
      cc_tok <- cc_tok[order(cc_tok$date), ]
      ec_line(cc_tok$date, list(list(name = "Claude", type = "line",
        data = I(cc_tok$claude), itemStyle = list(color = pal$claude))), "350px",
        y_name = "Tokens (M)")
    } else {
      gem_tok <- gem_tok[order(gem_tok$date), ]
      ec_line(gem_tok$date, list(list(name = "Gemini", type = "line",
        data = I(gem_tok$gemini), itemStyle = list(color = pal$gemini))), "350px",
        y_name = "Tokens (M)")
    }
  })

  output$token_comp <- renderUI({
    d <- cc_d_proj()
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(ec_empty())
    all_d <- sort(unique(d$date))
    val_at <- function(agg, col, dates) {
      vals <- rep(0, length(dates))
      vals[match(agg$date, dates)] <- agg[[col]]
      vals
    }
    series <- list()
    # Use whichever column names exist (cmonitor vs raw ccusage format)
    tok_cols <- list(
      Input = intersect(c("inputTokens"), names(d)),
      Output = intersect(c("outputTokens"), names(d)),
      `Cache Create` = intersect(c("cacheCreation", "cacheCreationTokens"), names(d)),
      `Cache Read` = intersect(c("cacheRead", "cacheReadTokens"), names(d))
    )
    for (nm in names(tok_cols)) {
      col <- tok_cols[[nm]]
      if (length(col) > 0 && col[1] %in% names(d)) {
        agg <- aggregate(d[[col[1]]] ~ date, data = d, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
        names(agg)[2] <- "val"
        series[[length(series) + 1]] <- list(
          name = nm, type = "bar", stack = "tokens",
          data = I(val_at(agg, "val", all_d))
        )
      }
    }
    if (length(series) == 0) return(ec_empty())
    ec_bar(all_d, series)
  })

  output$token_weekly <- renderUI({
    # W6 from #72: Weekly Tokens by Source — Claude, Gemini, Codex
    rows <- list()
    if (nrow(cc_d_proj()) > 0) {
      d <- cc_d_proj()
      d$week <- floor_date(d$date, "week")
      a <- aggregate(totalTokens ~ week, data = d, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
      a$source <- "Claude"
      rows[[1]] <- a
    }
    if (nrow(gem_d()) > 0) {
      d <- gem_d()
      d$week <- floor_date(d$date, "week")
      a <- aggregate(total_tokens ~ week, data = d, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
      names(a)[2] <- "totalTokens"
      a$source <- "Gemini"
      rows[[length(rows) + 1]] <- a
    }
    if (nrow(codex_d_proj()) > 0 && "total_tokens" %in% names(codex_d_proj())) {
      d <- codex_d_proj()
      d$week <- floor_date(d$date, "week")
      a <- aggregate(total_tokens ~ week, data = d, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
      names(a)[2] <- "totalTokens"
      a$source <- "Codex"
      rows[[length(rows) + 1]] <- a
    }
    if (length(rows) == 0) return(ec_empty())
    d <- do.call(rbind, rows)
    all_weeks <- sort(unique(d$week))
    sources <- unique(d$source)
    src_color <- c(Claude = SOURCE_PALETTE[["claude"]],
                   Gemini = SOURCE_PALETTE[["gemini"]],
                   Codex  = SOURCE_PALETTE[["codex"]])
    series <- lapply(sources, function(s) {
      sd <- d[d$source == s, ]
      vals <- rep(0, length(all_weeks))
      vals[match(sd$week, all_weeks)] <- sd$totalTokens
      list(name = s, type = "scatter", symbol = "circle", symbolSize = 10,
           data = I(vals),
           itemStyle = list(color = unname(src_color[s])))
    })
    ec_bar(all_weeks, series)
  })

  # --- Sessions ---------------------------------------------------------------
  # Summary value boxes
  # Helper: filter u_sessions by time cutoff + selected_projects
  sess_filtered <- reactive({
    if (!has_rows(u_sessions)) return(data.frame())
    d <- u_sessions[u_sessions$date >= cutoff(), ]
    sel <- resolve_sel(input$selected_projects)
    if (!is.null(sel) && "project_short" %in% names(d))
      d <- d[d$project_short %in% sel, ]
    d
  })

  output$vb_sess_count <- renderText({
    as.character(nrow(sess_filtered()))
  })
  output$vb_sess_duration <- renderText({
    d <- sess_filtered()
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return("0 min")
    total <- sum(d$duration_min, na.rm = TRUE)
    if (total >= 60) sprintf("%.1f hr", total / 60) else sprintf("%.0f min", total)
  })
  output$vb_sess_avg <- renderText({
    d <- sess_filtered()
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return("0 min")
    avg <- mean(d$duration_min, na.rm = TRUE)
    sprintf("%.1f min", avg)
  })

  output$sess_claude <- renderDT({
    d <- sess_filtered()
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(datatable(data.frame(Message = "No sessions for selected projects")))
    d <- d[order(d$started_at, decreasing = TRUE), ]
    d <- head(d, input$top_n)
    out <- data.frame(
      Project = if ("project_short" %in% names(d)) d$project_short else d$project,
      Started = paste(d$date, d$time_str),  # Use pre-extracted time
      `Duration (min)` = ifelse(is.na(d$duration_min), "—", sprintf("%.0f", d$duration_min)),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
      check.names = FALSE
    )
    datatable(out, options = list(dom = "t", pageLength = 20, scrollX = TRUE), rownames = FALSE)
  })

  output$sess_gemini <- renderDT({
    if (!has_rows(gem_sess)) return(datatable(data.frame()))
    d <- gem_sess[!is.na(gem_sess$date) & gem_sess$date >= cutoff(), ]
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(datatable(data.frame(Message = "No Gemini sessions in selected time horizon")))
    d <- d[order(d$total_cost, decreasing = TRUE), ]
    d <- head(d, input$top_n)
    out <- data.frame(
      Session = d$sessionId,
      Project = d$project,
      Cost = sprintf("$%.2f", d$total_cost),
      Tokens = format(d$total_tokens, big.mark = ","),
      Started = d$start_time,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
    datatable(out, options = list(dom = "t", pageLength = 20, scrollX = TRUE), rownames = FALSE)
  })

  output$sess_hist <- renderUI({
    d <- sess_filtered()
    d <- d[!is.na(d$duration_min) & d$duration_min > 0, ]
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(ec_empty("No sessions for selected projects"))
    h <- hist(d$duration_min, breaks = 20, plot = FALSE)
    ec_bar(round(h$mids, 1), list(
      list(name = "Sessions", type = "bar", data = I(h$counts),
           itemStyle = list(color = pal$hist))))
  })

  # sess_scatter migrated to DuckDB-WASM (#83 Phase 2): observe sends "duckdb_sessions_time"
  # message to JS queryAndRenderSessionsTime(), which queries sessions.parquet directly.
  observe({
    req(input$tabs == "Usage", input$usage_tabs == "Sessions")
    sel <- resolve_sel(input$selected_projects)
    projects_json <- if (is.null(sel)) list()
                     else if (length(sel) == 0L) "__NONE__"
                     else as.list(sel)
    cutoff_str <- if (!is.null(cutoff())) format(cutoff(), "%Y-%m-%d") else NULL
    session$sendCustomMessage("duckdb_sessions_time", list(
      projects = projects_json,
      min_date = cutoff_str
    ))
  })

  # --- By Project -------------------------------------------------------------
  # Session-weighted estimated cost by project
  proj_filtered <- reactive({
    if (!has_rows(cost_proj)) return(data.frame())
    d <- cost_proj
    d$date <- as.Date(d$date)
    d <- d[d$date >= cutoff(), ]
    # project column already normalised to canonical_project at load time
    d$project_short <- d$project
    # Apply project filter from master list
    sel <- resolve_sel(input$selected_projects)
    if (!is.null(sel))
      d <- d[d$project_short %in% sel, ]
    d
  })

  output$proj_cost_total <- renderUI({
    d <- proj_filtered()
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(ec_empty())
    # Aggregate total cost by project
    agg <- aggregate(est_cost ~ project_short, data = d, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
    agg <- agg[order(agg$est_cost, decreasing = FALSE), ]  # Ascending for dot chart
    # Cleveland dot chart (horizontal)
    ec(list(
      tooltip = list(trigger = "axis", axisPointer = list(type = "shadow")),
      grid = list(left = 120, containLabel = FALSE),
      xAxis = list(type = "value", name = "Est. Cost ($)"),
      yAxis = list(type = "category", data = I(agg$project_short)),
      series = list(list(
        type = "scatter",
        data = I(round(agg$est_cost, 2)),
        symbolSize = 12,
        itemStyle = list(color = pal$claude)
      ))
    ), h = "300px", aria_label = "Cleveland dot chart: estimated cost by project")
  })

  observe({
    req(input$tabs == "Usage", input$usage_tabs == "By Project")
    sel <- resolve_sel(input$selected_projects)
    # NULL        => All projects: send null  (JS omits IN filter, includes live projects)
    # character(0)=> None selected: send "__NONE__" sentinel (JS renders empty chart)
    # character[] => specific selection: send as JSON array
    projects_json <- if (is.null(sel)) list()
                     else if (length(sel) == 0L) "__NONE__"
                     else as.list(sel)
    cutoff_str <- if (!is.null(cutoff())) format(cutoff(), "%Y-%m-%d") else NULL
    session$sendCustomMessage("duckdb_sessions", list(
      projects = projects_json,
      min_date = cutoff_str
    ))
    # Phase 5 (#83): Session Duration by Project migrated to DuckDB-WASM
    session$sendCustomMessage("duckdb_sessions_duration", list(
      projects = projects_json,
      min_date = cutoff_str
    ))
  })

  output$proj_cost_daily <- renderUI({
    d <- proj_filtered()
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(ec_empty())
    # Get top N projects by total cost
    totals <- aggregate(est_cost ~ project_short, data = d, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
    top_proj <- head(totals[order(totals$est_cost, decreasing = TRUE), "project_short"], 8)
    d <- d[d$project_short %in% top_proj, ]
    # Pivot to wide format for stacked area
    all_dates <- sort(unique(d$date))
    series <- lapply(top_proj, function(p) {
      p_data <- d[d$project_short == p, c("date", "est_cost")]
      # Merge with all dates to fill gaps
      merged <- merge(data.frame(date = all_dates), p_data, by = "date", all.x = TRUE)
      merged$est_cost[is.na(merged$est_cost)] <- 0
      list(
        name = p, type = "line", stack = "total", areaStyle = list(),
        data = I(round(merged$est_cost[order(merged$date)], 2))
      )
    })
    ec_line(all_dates, series, h = "350px", y_name = "Cost ($)",
            aria_label = "Stacked area chart: daily estimated cost by project")
  })

  output$proj_cost_cumulative <- renderUI({
    d <- proj_filtered()
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(ec_empty())
    totals <- aggregate(est_cost ~ project_short, data = d, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
    top_proj <- head(totals[order(totals$est_cost, decreasing = TRUE), "project_short"], 8)
    d <- d[d$project_short %in% top_proj, ]
    all_dates <- sort(unique(d$date))
    series <- lapply(top_proj, function(p) {
      p_data <- d[d$project_short == p, c("date", "est_cost")]
      merged <- merge(data.frame(date = all_dates), p_data, by = "date", all.x = TRUE)
      merged$est_cost[is.na(merged$est_cost)] <- 0
      merged <- merged[order(merged$date), ]
      list(name = p, type = "line", data = I(round(cumsum(merged$est_cost), 2)))
    })
    ec_line(all_dates, series, h = "350px", y_name = "Cumulative Cost ($)",
            aria_label = "Cumulative estimated cost by project")
  })

  # proj_duration migrated to DuckDB-WASM (#83 Phase 5): observer sends "duckdb_sessions_duration"
  # message to JS queryAndRenderDuration(), which queries sessions.parquet directly.
  # SQL: SELECT canonical_project, ROUND(SUM(duration_min), 1) AS total_min
  #      FROM sessions.parquet WHERE ... GROUP BY canonical_project ORDER BY total_min DESC

  output$proj_share_heatmap <- renderUI({
    d <- proj_filtered()
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(ec_empty())
    # Get top 10 projects by total cost
    totals <- aggregate(est_cost ~ project_short, data = d, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
    top_proj <- head(totals[order(totals$est_cost, decreasing = TRUE), "project_short"], 10)
    d <- d[d$project_short %in% top_proj, ]
    # Convert to heatmap data: [x_index, y_index, value]
    dates <- sort(unique(d$date))
    projs <- top_proj
    heat_data <- list()
    for (i in seq_along(dates)) {
      for (j in seq_along(projs)) {
        val <- d[d$date == dates[i] & d$project_short == projs[j], "share"]
        val <- if (length(val) == 0) 0 else round(val[1] * 100, 1)
        heat_data[[length(heat_data) + 1]] <- list(i - 1, j - 1, val)
      }
    }
    # Build spec without JS() wrappers to avoid JS_EVAL serialization error.
    # The tooltip formatter is injected as a raw JS literal after toJSON().
    spec <- list(
      tooltip = list(position = "top", formatter = "__SHARE_FMT__"),
      grid = list(left = 100, bottom = 60, containLabel = FALSE),
      xAxis = list(type = "category", data = I(as.character(dates)), splitArea = list(show = TRUE)),
      yAxis = list(type = "category", data = I(projs), splitArea = list(show = TRUE)),
      visualMap = list(min = 0, max = 100, calculable = TRUE,
                       orient = "horizontal", left = "center", bottom = 5,
                       inRange = list(color = c("#0d1117", "#238636", "#40c463"))),
      series = list(list(
        name = "Share", type = "heatmap", data = heat_data,
        emphasis = list(itemStyle = list(shadowBlur = 10, shadowColor = "rgba(0,0,0,0.5)"))
      ))
    )
    id <- paste0("ec", sample.int(1e7, 1))
    # Use to_chart_json so any embedded JS_EVAL is handled centrally; the
    # remaining "__SHARE_FMT__" placeholder uses the same swap-then-gsub idiom.
    j <- to_chart_json(spec)
    j <- gsub('"__SHARE_FMT__"', 'function(p){return p.value[2]+"% share"}', j, fixed = TRUE)
    tagList(
      tags$div(id = id, class = "echart-div", role = "button",
               `aria-label` = "Heatmap: project cost share percentage by date",
               tabindex = "0", style = "width:100%;height:300px;cursor:zoom-in"),
      tags$script(HTML(paste0(
        "(function(){var c=echarts.init(document.getElementById('", id, "'),'dark');",
        "c.setOption(", j, ");new ResizeObserver(function(){c.resize()})",
        ".observe(document.getElementById('", id, "'));})();"
      )))
    )
  })

  # --- Phase 2: Weekly Commit Heatmap (By Project tab) -----------------------
  output$proj_weekly_heatmap <- renderUI({
    if (!has_rows(weekly_commits)) return(ec_empty("No weekly commit data available"))
    d <- weekly_commits
    # Filter to selected repos if sidebar has a selection
    if (!is.null(input$commit_project) && input$commit_project != "all" &&
        "project" %in% names(d)) {
      d <- d[d$project == input$commit_project, ]
    }
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(ec_empty("No data for selected repo"))
    ec_heatmap(d, x = "iso_week", y = "project", value = "n_commits",
               h = "350px",
               aria_label = "Weekly commit heatmap: commit counts by week and project")
  })

  # --- Phase 5 (#83): Cost-per-Commit Trend migrated to DuckDB-WASM ----------
  # output$cost_per_commit_chart renderUI removed — replaced by queryAndRenderCostPerCommit()
  # JS function that queries costs.parquet JOIN git_commits.parquet client-side.
  # Fixed div#cost_per_commit_ec is in the UI. Observer below triggers re-query
  # when the Cost Trends tab is active and filter/granularity inputs change.
  observe({
    req(input$tabs == "Usage", input$usage_tabs == "Cost Trends")
    sel <- resolve_sel(input$selected_projects)
    projects_json <- if (is.null(sel)) list()
                     else if (length(sel) == 0L) "__NONE__"
                     else as.list(sel)
    cutoff_str <- if (!is.null(cutoff())) format(cutoff(), "%Y-%m-%d") else NULL
    gran <- if (!is.null(input$granularity)) input$granularity else "weekly"
    session$sendCustomMessage("duckdb_cost_per_commit", list(
      projects    = projects_json,
      min_date    = cutoff_str,
      granularity = gran
    ))
  })

  # --- Phase 2: File Churn DataTable (Git tab) --------------------------------
  output$file_churn_table <- renderDT({
    if (!has_rows(file_churn_data)) return(datatable(data.frame(Message = "No file churn data")))
    d <- file_churn_data
    # Filter to selected repo if applicable
    if (!is.null(input$commit_project) && input$commit_project != "all" &&
        "project" %in% names(d)) {
      d <- d[d$project == input$commit_project, ]
    }
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(datatable(data.frame(Message = "No data for selected repo")))
    # Select display columns
    cols <- intersect(c("project", "file", "n_commits", "total_lines_changed", "last_changed_date"),
                      names(d))
    out <- d[order(d$total_lines_changed, decreasing = TRUE), cols, drop = FALSE]
    # Shorten file path for display
    out$file <- basename(out$file)
    datatable(out,
      caption = "File churn by lines changed (top files per project)",
      options = list(
        pageLength = 15, dom = "ftip", scrollX = TRUE, scrollY = "350px",
        order = list(list(which(cols == "total_lines_changed") - 1L, "desc"))
      ),
      rownames = FALSE
    )
  })

  # --- Phase 2: Change Coupling Force Graph (Git tab) -------------------------
  output$change_coupling_graph <- renderUI({
    if (!has_rows(change_coupling_data)) return(ec_empty("No coupling data available"))
    d <- change_coupling_data
    # Filter to selected repo if applicable
    if (!is.null(input$commit_project) && input$commit_project != "all" &&
        "project" %in% names(d)) {
      d <- d[d$project == input$commit_project, ]
    }
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(ec_empty("No coupling data for selected repo"))
    # Cap to top 60 pairs to keep graph readable
    d <- head(d[order(d$weight_normalised, decreasing = TRUE), ], 60)

    # Build unique nodes
    all_files <- unique(c(d$file_a, d$file_b))
    proj_lookup <- character(length(all_files))
    names(proj_lookup) <- all_files
    for (f in all_files) {
      rows_a <- d[d$file_a == f, "project"]
      rows_b <- d[d$file_b == f, "project"]
      proj_lookup[f] <- c(rows_a, rows_b)[1]
    }
    cat_map <- c("llm" = 0L, "llmtelemetry" = 1L)
    # (#888) Use full repo-relative path as node id to avoid collisions when
    # two files share the same basename (e.g. R/utils.R and tests/utils.R).
    # basename() is kept only as the human-readable display label.
    # ECharts graph series matches edge source/target to node id when id is
    # provided, so edges also use full paths as identifiers.
    nodes <- lapply(all_files, function(f) {
      cat_idx <- if (!is.na(proj_lookup[f]) && proj_lookup[f] %in% names(cat_map)) {
        cat_map[[proj_lookup[f]]]
      } else {
        2L
      }
      list(name = f, value = 1,
           symbolSize = 10,
           category = cat_idx,
           label = list(formatter = basename(f)))
    })

    edges <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(d)), function(i) {
      w <- d$weight_normalised[i]
      list(source = d$file_a[i],
           target = d$file_b[i],
           n_cochanges = d$n_cochanges[i],
           lineStyle = list(width = max(1, round(w * 5)),
                            opacity = max(0.3, w)))
    })

    ec_force_graph(nodes, edges, h = "450px",
      aria_label = "Force-directed graph: files that change together. Node = file, edge = co-change count.")
  })

  # --- Time Blocks ------------------------------------------------------------
  blk <- reactive({
    if (!has_rows(cc_blocks)) return(data.frame())
    d <- cc_blocks[cc_blocks$date >= (date_max - input$block_days), ]
    # (#277 sub-task E): filter provider-error rows so they don't inflate
    # cost/token aggregates. These are ccusage blocks where the API returned a
    # provider-side error (e.g. rate limit, server error) and recorded 0 usage.
    # The field may be named error_kind or errorKind depending on ccusage version.
    if ("error_kind" %in% names(d))
      d <- d[is.na(d$error_kind) | d$error_kind != "provider", , drop = FALSE]
    if ("errorKind" %in% names(d))
      d <- d[is.na(d$errorKind) | d$errorKind != "provider", , drop = FALSE]
    d
  })

  output$block_table <- renderDT({
    d <- blk()
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(datatable(data.frame()))
    # Sort most-recent-first so display order is newest at top
    d <- d[order(d$startTime, decreasing = TRUE), ]
    n <- nrow(d)
    eff_end <- coalesce2(d$actualEndTime, d$endTime)
    out <- data.frame(
      Day = format(d$date, "%a %Y-%m-%d"),
      Start = format(d$startTime, "%H:%M"),
      End = format(eff_end, "%H:%M"),
      Duration = sprintf("%.1fh", d$duration_hrs),
      Cost = sprintf("$%.2f", d$costUSD),
      `$/hr` = sprintf("$%.2f", d$cost_per_hr),
      Tokens = fmt_tokens(if ("totalTokens" %in% names(d)) d$totalTokens else rep(0, n)),
      `Tok/hr` = fmt_tokens(if ("tok_per_hr" %in% names(d)) d$tok_per_hr else rep(0, n)),
      Models = if ("models" %in% names(d)) clean_model(d$models) else rep("-", n),
      check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
    datatable(out,
      extensions = "RowGroup",
      options = list(
        pageLength = 50, dom = "rtip", scrollX = TRUE,
        rowGroup = list(
          dataSrc = 0,
          startRender = DT::JS("function(rows, group) {
            var dur=0, cost=0, tok=0;
            function pTok(s){s=s.trim();if(s.endsWith('B'))return parseFloat(s)*1e9;if(s.endsWith('M'))return parseFloat(s)*1e6;if(s.endsWith('k'))return parseFloat(s)*1e3;return parseInt(s.replace(/,/g,''))||0;}
            function fTok(n){if(n>=1e9)return(n/1e9).toFixed(1)+'B';if(n>=1e6)return(n/1e6).toFixed(1)+'M';if(n>=1e3)return Math.round(n/1e3)+'k';return n.toString();}
            rows.every(function() {
              var d=this.data();
              dur += parseFloat(d[3]) || 0;
              cost += parseFloat(d[4].replace('$','')) || 0;
              tok += pTok(d[6]);
            });
            return $('<tr style=\"font-weight:bold;background:#2a2a2a\"/>')
              .append('<td colspan=\"2\">' + group + '</td>')
              .append('<td>' + dur.toFixed(1) + 'h</td>')
              .append('<td>$' + cost.toFixed(2) + '</td>')
              .append('<td colspan=\"2\">' + fTok(tok) + ' tok</td>')
              .append('<td colspan=\"2\">' + rows.count() + ' blocks</td>');
          }")
        ),
        columnDefs = list(
          list(visible = FALSE, targets = 0),
          list(targets = 8, render = DT::JS(
            "function(data, type, row) { return '<span style=\"white-space:nowrap\">' + data + '</span>'; }"
          ))
        ),
        order = list()  # preserve server-side sort (d$startTime decreasing) — #896
      ),
      rownames = FALSE)
  })

  output$block_timeline <- renderUI({
    d <- blk()
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(ec_empty())
    d <- d[order(d$startTime), ]
    pts <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(d)), function(i)
      list(round(d$costUSD[i], 2), round(d$duration_hrs[i], 2), round(d$costUSD[i], 2)))
    id <- paste0("ec", sample.int(1e7, 1))
    opt <- list(
      tooltip = list(trigger = "item",
        formatter = "{c}"),
      grid = list(containLabel = TRUE),
      xAxis = list(type = "value", name = "Cost ($)"),
      yAxis = list(type = "value", name = "Duration (h)"),
      series = list(list(type = "scatter", data = pts,
                         itemStyle = list(color = pal$claude)))
    )
    j <- to_chart_json(opt)
    tagList(
      tags$div(id = id, class = "echart-div", role = "button",
               `aria-label` = "Cost vs duration scatter chart — click to enlarge",
               tabindex = "0", style = "width:100%;height:300px;cursor:zoom-in"),
      tags$script(HTML(paste0(
        "(function(){var c=echarts.init(document.getElementById('", id, "'),'dark');",
        "var o=", j, ";",
        "o.series[0].symbolSize=function(d){return Math.max(3,Math.sqrt(d[2])*4)};",
        "o.tooltip.formatter=function(p){return '$'+p.value[0].toFixed(2)+' | '+p.value[1].toFixed(1)+'h'};",
        "c.setOption(o);new ResizeObserver(function(){c.resize()})",
        ".observe(document.getElementById('", id, "'));",
        "function fs(){var url=c.getDataURL({type:'png',pixelRatio:2,backgroundColor:'#000'});",
        "var ov=document.createElement('div');ov.className='fullscreen-overlay';",
        "var img=document.createElement('img');img.src=url;ov.appendChild(img);",
        "ov.onclick=function(){ov.remove()};document.body.appendChild(ov)}",
        "document.getElementById('", id, "').addEventListener('click',fs);",
        "document.getElementById('", id, "').addEventListener('keydown',function(e){",
        "if(e.key==='Enter'||e.key===' '){e.preventDefault();fs()}})})();"
      )))
    )
  })

  output$block_heatmap <- renderUI({
    d <- blk()
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(ec_empty())
    d$wday_chr <- as.character(d$wday)
    heat_agg <- aggregate(costUSD ~ wday_chr + hour, data = d, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
    day_names <- c("Mon", "Tue", "Wed", "Thu", "Fri", "Sat", "Sun")
    all_combos <- expand.grid(hour = 0:23, wday_chr = day_names, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
    heat <- merge(all_combos, heat_agg, by = c("wday_chr", "hour"), all.x = TRUE)
    heat$costUSD[is.na(heat$costUSD)] <- 0
    heat_data <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(heat)), function(i)
      list(heat$hour[i], match(heat$wday_chr[i], day_names) - 1, round(heat$costUSD[i], 2)))
    max_cost <- max(heat$costUSD, na.rm = TRUE)
    ec(list(
      tooltip = list(trigger = "item"),
      grid = list(containLabel = TRUE),
      xAxis = list(type = "category", data = I(0:23), name = "Hour"),
      yAxis = list(type = "category", data = I(day_names)),
      visualMap = list(min = 0, max = max(max_cost, 1), calculable = TRUE,
        inRange = list(color = I(c("#1a1a1a", pal$orange, pal$gemini))),
        textStyle = list(color = "#ccc")),
      series = list(list(type = "heatmap", data = heat_data,
        label = list(show = FALSE)))
    ))
  })

  # --- Commits -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # Populate project filter from the multi-project data
  observe({
    if (!has_rows(git_commits_proj)) return()
    projs <- sort(unique(git_commits_proj$project))
    updateSelectInput(session, "commit_project",
      choices = c("All repos" = "all", setNames(projs, projs)),
      selected = "all")
  })

  commits_filt <- reactive({
    d <- if (has_rows(git_commits_proj)) git_commits_proj else git_commits
    if (!has_rows(d)) return(data.frame())
    d <- d[!is.na(d$date) & d$date >= cutoff(), ]
    proj_sel <- input$commit_project
    if (!is.null(proj_sel) && proj_sel != "all" && "project" %in% names(d))
      d <- d[d$project == proj_sel, ]
    d
  })

  output$commits_by_proj <- renderDT({
    d <- commits_filt()  # already filtered by cutoff() + commit_project (#815/#816)
    if (!has_rows(d)) return(DT::datatable(data.frame()))
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(DT::datatable(data.frame()))
    proj_col <- if ("project" %in% names(d)) d$project else rep("llmtelemetry", nrow(d))
    splits   <- split(d, proj_col)
    if (length(splits) == 0) return(DT::datatable(data.frame()))
    summary_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(splits), function(i) {
      g <- splits[[i]]
      if (is.null(g) || nrow(g) == 0) return(NULL)
      lc  <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(g$lines_changed))
      la  <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(g$lines_added))
      ld  <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(g$lines_deleted))
      med <- if (any(!is.na(lc))) round(median(lc, na.rm = TRUE)) else NA_real_
      mn  <- if (any(!is.na(lc))) round(mean(lc,   na.rm = TRUE)) else NA_real_
      data.frame(
        Project     = names(splits)[[i]],
        Commits     = nrow(g),
        Med_Lines   = med,
        Mean_Lines  = mn,
        Lines_Added = sum(la, na.rm = TRUE),
        Lines_Del   = sum(ld, na.rm = TRUE),
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
    }))
    if (is.null(summary_df) || nrow(summary_df) == 0) return(DT::datatable(data.frame()))
    summary_df <- summary_df[order(summary_df$Commits, decreasing = TRUE), ]
    DT::datatable(summary_df,
      rownames = FALSE,
      caption = "Commit activity by project (selected time window)",
      options = list(pageLength = 10, dom = "t", order = list(list(1, "desc"))),
      class = "compact stripe"
    )
  })

  output$vb_commits <- renderText({
    d <- commits_filt()
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return("0")
    as.character(nrow(d))
  })
  output$vb_median_lines <- renderText({
    d <- commits_filt()
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return("0")
    as.character(round(median(d$lines_changed, na.rm = TRUE)))
  })
  output$vb_mean_lines <- renderText({
    d <- commits_filt()
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return("0")
    as.character(round(mean(d$lines_changed, na.rm = TRUE)))
  })

  output$commit_hist <- renderUI({
    d <- commits_filt()
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(ec_empty(h = "350px"))
    h <- hist(d$lines_changed, breaks = 30, plot = FALSE)
    ec_bar(round(h$mids), list(
      list(name = "Commits", type = "bar", data = I(h$counts),
           itemStyle = list(color = pal$hist))), "350px")
  })

  output$commit_scatter_time <- renderUI({
    d <- commits_filt()
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(ec_empty())
    pts <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(d)), function(i)
      list(as.character(d$date[i]), d$lines_changed[i], d$files_changed[i]))
    ec_scatter(pts, pal$claude, x_type = "time", size_col = 3, scale = 3)
  })

  output$commit_scatter_add_del <- renderUI({
    d <- commits_filt()
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(ec_empty())
    pts <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(d)), function(i)
      list(d$lines_added[i], d$lines_deleted[i], d$files_changed[i]))
    ec_scatter(pts, pal$gemini, size_col = 3, scale = 3)
  })

  # --- Repo Health ------------------------------------------------------------
  bus_dot <- function(d, label = "Bus factor dot chart") {
    if (!has_rows(d)) return(ec_empty())
    d <- head(d[order(d$commits, decreasing = TRUE), ], 10)
    d <- d[nrow(d):1, ]  # reverse for bottom-up display
    total <- sum(d$commits)
    labels <- paste0(d$author, " (", round(d$commits / total * 100), "%)")
    ec(list(
      tooltip = list(trigger = "axis"),
      toolbox = list(feature = list(
        dataZoom = list(), restore = list(), saveAsImage = list()
      )),
      dataZoom = list(list(type = "inside", xAxisIndex = 0)),
      grid = list(containLabel = TRUE, left = 120),
      xAxis = list(type = "value", name = "Commits"),
      yAxis = list(type = "category", data = I(labels)),
      series = list(list(type = "scatter", data = I(d$commits),
        symbol = "circle", symbolSize = 14,
        itemStyle = list(color = pal$claude),
        label = list(show = TRUE, position = "right", color = "#ccc",
                     distance = 8)))
    ), aria_label = label)
  }
  output$rh_bus_all <- renderUI(bus_dot(git_bus$all_time, "Bus factor all time: contributor commit share"))
  output$rh_bus_6mo <- renderUI(bus_dot(git_bus$recent_6mo, "Bus factor 6 months: recent contributor concentration"))

  output$rh_velocity <- renderUI({
    if (!has_rows(git_vel)) return(ec_empty())
    d <- git_vel[order(git_vel$month), ]
    ec_line(d$month, list(
      list(name = "Commits", type = "line", data = I(d$commits),
           itemStyle = list(color = pal$claude),
           areaStyle = list(opacity = 0.3))),
      y_name = "Commits")
  })

  output$rh_timing <- renderUI({
    if (!has_rows(git_timing)) return(ec_empty())
    d <- git_timing
    day_names <- c("Mon", "Tue", "Wed", "Thu", "Fri", "Sat", "Sun")
    if (!"day_name" %in% names(d)) d$day_name <- day_names[d$weekday]
    heat_data <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(d)), function(i)
      list(as.integer(d$hour[i]), match(d$day_name[i], day_names) - 1,
           as.integer(d$commits[i])))
    max_c <- max(d$commits, na.rm = TRUE)
    ec(list(
      tooltip = list(trigger = "item"),
      grid = list(containLabel = TRUE),
      xAxis = list(type = "category", data = I(0:23), name = "Hour"),
      yAxis = list(type = "category", data = I(day_names)),
      visualMap = list(min = 0, max = max(max_c, 1), calculable = TRUE,
        inRange = list(color = I(c("#1a1a1a", pal$accent, pal$claude))),
        textStyle = list(color = "#ccc")),
      series = list(list(type = "heatmap", data = heat_data,
        label = list(show = FALSE)))
    ), aria_label = "Commit timing heatmap: when commits happen by hour and day")
  })

  output$rh_file_growth <- renderUI({
    if (!has_rows(git_file_growth)) return(ec_empty("No file growth data available"))
    d <- git_file_growth
    # Parse date
    d$date <- as.Date(d$date)
    # These are 6-month rolling aggregates, show all snapshots
    # Aggregate by date (sum across projects if multiple)
    agg <- aggregate(cbind(files_added, files_deleted) ~ date, data = d, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
    agg <- agg[order(agg$date), ]

    if (nrow(agg) == 0) return(ec_empty("No data available"))

    # Create line chart with two series
    series <- list(
      list(name = "Added (6mo)", type = "line", data = I(agg$files_added),
           itemStyle = list(color = pal$accent),
           areaStyle = list(opacity = 0.3)),
      list(name = "Deleted (6mo)", type = "line", data = I(agg$files_deleted),
           itemStyle = list(color = pal$gemini),
           areaStyle = list(opacity = 0.3))
    )
    ec_line(agg$date, series, y_name = "Files (6-month rolling)",
            aria_label = "6-month rolling file growth: added vs deleted")
  })

  output$rh_churn <- renderUI({
    if (!has_rows(git_churn)) return(ec_empty())
    d <- head(git_churn[order(git_churn$changes, decreasing = TRUE), ], 15)
    d <- d[nrow(d):1, ]  # reverse for horizontal dotchart
    ec(list(
      tooltip = list(trigger = "axis"),
      toolbox = list(feature = list(
        dataZoom = list(), restore = list(), saveAsImage = list()
      )),
      dataZoom = list(list(type = "inside", xAxisIndex = 0)),
      grid = list(containLabel = TRUE, left = 150),
      xAxis = list(type = "value", name = "Changes"),
      yAxis = list(type = "category", data = I(basename(d$file))),
      series = list(list(type = "scatter", data = I(d$changes),
        symbol = "circle", symbolSize = 12,
        itemStyle = list(color = pal$orange),
        label = list(show = TRUE, position = "right", color = "#ccc",
                     distance = 6)))
    ), aria_label = "Churn hotspots: most-modified files in last 6 months")
  })

  output$rh_bugs <- renderUI({
    if (!has_rows(git_bugs)) return(ec_empty())
    d <- head(git_bugs[order(git_bugs$fixes, decreasing = TRUE), ], 15)
    d <- d[nrow(d):1, ]
    ec(list(
      tooltip = list(trigger = "axis"),
      toolbox = list(feature = list(
        dataZoom = list(), restore = list(), saveAsImage = list()
      )),
      dataZoom = list(list(type = "inside", xAxisIndex = 0)),
      grid = list(containLabel = TRUE, left = 150),
      xAxis = list(type = "value", name = "Fixes"),
      yAxis = list(type = "category", data = I(basename(d$file))),
      series = list(list(type = "scatter", data = I(d$fixes),
        symbol = "circle", symbolSize = 12,
        itemStyle = list(color = pal$gemini),
        label = list(show = TRUE, position = "right", color = "#ccc",
                     distance = 6)))
    ), aria_label = "Bug fixes by file: files with most fix commits")
  })

  output$rh_todo <- renderDT({
    if (!has_rows(git_todo)) return(datatable(data.frame()))
    datatable(git_todo, options = list(dom = "t", pageLength = 15, scrollX = TRUE),
      rownames = FALSE)
  })

  output$rh_crisis <- renderDT({
    if (!has_rows(git_crisis)) return(datatable(data.frame()))
    datatable(git_crisis, options = list(dom = "t", pageLength = 10, scrollX = TRUE),
      rownames = FALSE)
  })

  output$gh_events_chart <- renderUI({
    if (!has_rows(gh_events)) {
      return(ec_empty("No GitHub events data available"))
    }

    # Parse dates and aggregate by date and event type using base R
    gh_events$date <- as.Date(substr(gh_events$created_at, 1, 10))

    # Count events by date and type using aggregate
    event_summary <- aggregate(
      created_at ~ date + event_type,
      data = gh_events,
      FUN = length
    )
    names(event_summary)[3] <- "n"

    if (nrow(event_summary) == 0) {
      return(ec_empty("No events in selected period"))
    }

    # Get unique dates and event types
    all_dates <- sort(unique(event_summary$date))
    event_types <- unique(event_summary$event_type)

    # Create series for each event type
    series <- lapply(event_types, function(et) {
      counts <- sapply(all_dates, function(d) {
        idx <- event_summary$date == d & event_summary$event_type == et
        if (any(idx)) event_summary$n[idx][1] else 0
      })
      list(
        name = et,
        type = "bar",
        stack = "events",
        data = I(counts)
      )
    })

    ec_bar(all_dates, series, h = "350px", y_name = "Events",
      aria_label = "GitHub issue events by type over time")
  })

  # --- Calibration ------------------------------------------------------------
  output$cal_reliability <- renderUI({
    if (!has_rows(preds_raw)) return(ec_empty())
    # Recompute reliability buckets from preds_raw filtered by cutoff (#82)
    d <- preds_raw[!is.na(preds_raw$outcome) & !is.na(preds_raw$p_success), ]
    if (!is.null(cutoff()) && "recorded_at" %in% names(d)) {
      rat <- tryCatch(as.Date(as.character(d$recorded_at)), error = function(e) NULL)
      if (!is.null(rat)) d <- d[!is.na(rat) & rat >= cutoff(), ]
    }
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(ec_empty("No predictions in this period"))
    d$outcome_num <- ifelse(d$outcome == TRUE | d$outcome == "true", 1,
                    ifelse(d$outcome == "partial", 0.5, 0))
    d$bucket_cut <- cut(d$p_success, breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2), include.lowest = TRUE)
    agg_p <- aggregate(p_success  ~ bucket_cut, data = d, FUN = mean, na.rm = TRUE)
    agg_a <- aggregate(outcome_num ~ bucket_cut, data = d, FUN = mean, na.rm = TRUE)
    agg_n <- aggregate(outcome_num ~ bucket_cut, data = d, FUN = length)
    d <- merge(merge(agg_p, agg_a, by = "bucket_cut"), agg_n, by = "bucket_cut")
    names(d) <- c("bucket_cut", "mean_predicted", "actual_accuracy", "n")
    # Scatter: predicted (x) vs actual (y) + diagonal reference line
    pts <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(d)), function(i)
      list(d$mean_predicted[i], d$actual_accuracy[i], d$n[i]))
    id <- paste0("ec", sample.int(1e7, 1))
    opt <- list(
      tooltip = list(trigger = "item"),
      toolbox = list(feature = list(
        dataZoom = list(), restore = list(), saveAsImage = list()
      )),
      dataZoom = list(
        list(type = "inside", xAxisIndex = 0),
        list(type = "inside", yAxisIndex = 0),
        list(type = "slider", xAxisIndex = 0, bottom = 5),
        list(type = "slider", yAxisIndex = 0, right = 5)
      ),
      grid = list(containLabel = TRUE, bottom = 50, right = 50),
      xAxis = list(type = "value", name = "Predicted", min = 0, max = 1),
      yAxis = list(type = "value", name = "Actual", min = 0, max = 1),
      series = list(
        list(type = "line", data = list(list(0, 0), list(1, 1)),
             lineStyle = list(type = "dashed", color = "#666"),
             symbol = "none", name = "Perfect"),
        list(type = "scatter", data = pts, name = "Buckets",
             itemStyle = list(color = pal$claude))
      )
    )
    j <- to_chart_json(opt)
    tagList(
      tags$div(id = id, class = "echart-div", style = "width:100%;height:340px"),
      tags$script(HTML(paste0(
        "(function(){var c=echarts.init(document.getElementById('", id, "'),'dark');",
        "var o=", j, ";",
        "o.series[1].symbolSize=function(d){return Math.max(8,Math.sqrt(d[2])*6)};",
        "o.series[1].label={show:true,formatter:function(p){return p.value[2]+' preds'},color:'#ccc',fontSize:10};",
        "c.setOption(o);new ResizeObserver(function(){c.resize()})",
        ".observe(document.getElementById('", id, "'))})();"
      )))
    )
  })

  output$cal_brier <- renderUI({
    if (!has_rows(preds_raw) || !"brier" %in% names(preds_raw)) return(ec_empty())
    d <- preds_raw[!is.na(preds_raw$outcome_recorded_at) & !is.na(preds_raw$brier), ]
    if (!is.null(cutoff())) {
      orat <- tryCatch(as.Date(as.character(d$outcome_recorded_at)), error = function(e) NULL)
      if (!is.null(orat)) d <- d[!is.na(orat) & orat >= cutoff(), ]
    }
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(ec_empty("No resolved predictions in this period"))
    d$orat_date <- tryCatch(as.Date(as.character(d$outcome_recorded_at)), error = function(e) as.Date(NA))
    gran <- if (!is.null(input$granularity)) input$granularity else "daily"
    if (gran == "monthly") {
      d$period <- format(d$orat_date, "%Y-%m")
    } else if (gran == "weekly") {
      d$period <- format(d$orat_date, "%Y-W%V")
    } else {
      d$period <- as.character(d$orat_date)
    }
    agg <- aggregate(brier ~ period, data = d, FUN = mean, na.rm = TRUE)
    agg <- agg[order(agg$period), ]
    ec_line(agg$period, list(
      list(name = "Mean Brier", type = "line", data = I(round(agg$brier, 4)),
           itemStyle = list(color = pal$claude),
           markLine = list(data = list(list(yAxis = 0.25, name = "Uninformative")),
             lineStyle = list(color = "#666", type = "dashed")))
    ), default_window_days = 0)
  })

  output$cal_by_type <- renderUI({
    if (!has_rows(preds_raw) || !"task_type" %in% names(preds_raw)) return(ec_empty())
    d <- preds_raw[!is.na(preds_raw$outcome), ]
    if (!is.null(cutoff()) && "outcome_recorded_at" %in% names(d)) {
      orat <- tryCatch(as.Date(as.character(d$outcome_recorded_at)), error = function(e) NULL)
      if (!is.null(orat)) d <- d[!is.na(orat) & orat >= cutoff(), ]
    }
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(ec_empty("No resolved predictions in this period"))
    d$outcome_num <- ifelse(d$outcome == TRUE | d$outcome == "true", 1,
                     ifelse(d$outcome == "partial", 0.5, 0))
    agg <- aggregate(cbind(accuracy = outcome_num, n = outcome_num) ~ task_type,
      data = d, FUN = function(x) c(mean(x), length(x)))
    if (is.matrix(agg$accuracy)) {
      types <- agg$task_type
      acc <- round(agg$accuracy[, 1], 2)
      counts <- as.integer(agg$n[, 2])
    } else {
      types <- agg$task_type
      acc <- round(agg$accuracy, 2)
      counts <- as.integer(agg$n)
    }
    labels <- paste0(types, " (n=", counts, ")")
    h_px <- max(280, length(labels) * 45)
    ec(list(
      tooltip = list(trigger = "axis"),
      toolbox = list(feature = list(
        dataZoom = list(), restore = list(), saveAsImage = list()
      )),
      dataZoom = list(
        list(type = "inside", xAxisIndex = 0),
        list(type = "inside", yAxisIndex = 0),
        list(type = "slider", yAxisIndex = 0, right = 5)
      ),
      grid = list(containLabel = TRUE, left = 150, right = 60, top = 20, bottom = 20),
      xAxis = list(type = "value", name = "Accuracy", min = 0, max = 1),
      yAxis = list(type = "category", data = I(labels)),
      series = list(list(type = "scatter", data = I(acc),
        symbol = "circle", symbolSize = 14,
        itemStyle = list(color = pal$accent),
        label = list(show = TRUE, position = "right", color = "#ccc")))
    ), h = paste0(h_px, "px"))
  })

  output$cal_by_project <- renderUI({
    if (!has_rows(preds_raw) || !"project_name" %in% names(preds_raw)) return(ec_empty("No project data"))
    d <- preds_raw[!is.na(preds_raw$outcome), ]
    if (!is.null(cutoff()) && "outcome_recorded_at" %in% names(d)) {
      orat <- tryCatch(as.Date(as.character(d$outcome_recorded_at)), error = function(e) NULL)
      if (!is.null(orat)) d <- d[!is.na(orat) & orat >= cutoff(), ]
    }
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(ec_empty("No resolved predictions in this period"))

    # Convert outcome to numeric
    d$outcome_num <- ifelse(d$outcome == TRUE | d$outcome == "true", 1,
                            ifelse(d$outcome == "partial", 0.5, 0))

    # Aggregate by project
    agg <- aggregate(cbind(accuracy = outcome_num, n = outcome_num) ~ project_name,
                     data = d, FUN = function(x) c(mean(x), length(x)))
    # Flatten the matrix columns
    agg_df <- data.frame(
      project = agg$project_name,
      accuracy = agg$accuracy[,1],
      n = agg$n[,2]
    )
    agg_df <- agg_df[order(agg_df$accuracy, decreasing = TRUE), ]

    # Cleveland dot plot
    acc_vals <- round(agg_df$accuracy * 100, 1)
    proj_labels <- agg_df$project
    h_px <- max(280, nrow(agg_df) * 45)
    ec(list(
      tooltip = list(trigger = "axis",
        formatter = "function(p){return p[0].name+': '+p[0].value+'%'}"),
      toolbox = list(feature = list(
        dataZoom = list(), restore = list(), saveAsImage = list()
      )),
      dataZoom = list(
        list(type = "inside", xAxisIndex = 0),
        list(type = "inside", yAxisIndex = 0),
        list(type = "slider", yAxisIndex = 0, right = 5)
      ),
      grid = list(containLabel = TRUE, left = 150, right = 60, top = 20, bottom = 20),
      xAxis = list(type = "value", min = 0, max = 100, name = "Accuracy %"),
      yAxis = list(type = "category", data = I(rev(proj_labels))),
      series = list(list(
        type = "scatter", data = I(rev(acc_vals)),
        symbol = "circle", symbolSize = 14,
        itemStyle = list(color = "#4daf4a"),
        label = list(show = TRUE, position = "right", color = "#ccc",
                     formatter = "function(p){return p.value+'%'}")
      ))
    ), h = paste0(h_px, "px"), aria_label = "Accuracy by project dotchart")
  })

  output$cal_log <- renderDT({
    if (!has_rows(preds_raw)) return(datatable(data.frame()))
    d <- preds_raw
    cols <- intersect(c("prediction_id", "project_name", "task_type",
      "task_description", "p_success", "outcome", "outcome_notes"), names(d))
    d <- d[, cols, drop = FALSE]
    datatable(d, options = list(dom = "ftip", pageLength = 15, scrollX = TRUE),
      rownames = FALSE)
  })

  # --- Cross-tool daily cost (Cost Trends sub-tab) ----------------------------
  # Panel 2 of #146: stacked/grouped daily cost by Claude, Codex, Gemini.
  # Reads from the already-loaded cc_daily, codex_daily, gem_daily data frames.
  # Degrades gracefully: any absent source is simply omitted from the chart.
  output$cross_tool_daily_cost <- renderUI({
    # (#281 Phase 3a): Claude series uses CodexBar cb_d() as primary.
    # Falls back to cc_d() when CodexBar absent.
    has_cb  <- has_rows(cb_d()) && "cost_usd" %in% names(cb_d())
    has_cc  <- !has_cb && has_rows(cc_d()) && "totalCost" %in% names(cc_d())
    has_cx  <- has_rows(codex_d()) && "est_cost_usd" %in% names(codex_d())
    has_gem <- has_rows(gem_d()) && "total_cost" %in% names(gem_d())
    if (!has_cb && !has_cc && !has_cx && !has_gem)
      return(ec_empty("No cross-tool cost data available", h = "300px"))

    rows <- list()
    if (has_cb) {
      a <- aggregate(cost_usd ~ date, data = cb_d(), FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
      names(a)[2] <- "cost"
      a$source <- "Claude"
      rows[[length(rows) + 1]] <- a
    } else if (has_cc) {
      a <- aggregate(totalCost ~ date, data = cc_d(), FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
      names(a)[2] <- "cost"
      a$source <- "Claude"
      rows[[length(rows) + 1]] <- a
    }
    if (has_cx) {
      a <- aggregate(est_cost_usd ~ date, data = codex_d(), FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
      names(a)[2] <- "cost"
      a$source <- "Codex"
      rows[[length(rows) + 1]] <- a
    }
    if (has_gem) {
      a <- aggregate(total_cost ~ date, data = gem_d(), FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
      names(a)[2] <- "cost"
      a$source <- "Gemini"
      rows[[length(rows) + 1]] <- a
    }
    if (length(rows) == 0) return(ec_empty("No cross-tool cost data available", h = "300px"))

    d <- do.call(rbind, rows)
    all_dates <- sort(unique(d$date))
    src_color <- c(Claude = SOURCE_PALETTE[["claude"]],
                   Codex  = SOURCE_PALETTE[["codex"]],
                   Gemini = SOURCE_PALETTE[["gemini"]])
    sources <- unique(d$source)
    series <- lapply(sources, function(s) {
      sd <- d[d$source == s, ]
      vals <- rep(0, length(all_dates))
      vals[match(sd$date, all_dates)] <- round(sd$cost, 4)
      list(name = s, type = "line",
           data = I(vals),
           areaStyle = list(opacity = 0.15),
           itemStyle = list(color = unname(src_color[s])),
           lineStyle  = list(color = unname(src_color[s])))
    })
    ec_line(all_dates, series, h = "300px", y_name = "Cost (USD)",
            aria_label = "Grouped line chart: daily API cost by tool (Claude via CodexBar, Codex, Gemini)")
  })

  # --- Validation Health card (2e): deficit_pct from unified_costs.json -------
  output$validation_health_card <- renderUI({
    uc <- u_costs
    if (!has_rows(uc) || !"deficit_pct" %in% names(uc))
      return(tags$div(class = "p-3 text-muted small",
        "deficit_pct not available — run export_dashboard_data.R with Phase 2c to populate."))
    # Filter to time horizon
    if (!is.null(cutoff()) && "date" %in% names(uc))
      uc <- uc[uc$date >= cutoff(), ]
    # Recent 30 rows for sparkline
    uc <- uc[order(uc$date), ]
    valid <- uc[!is.na(uc$deficit_pct), ]
    if (nrow(valid) == 0)
      return(tags$div(class = "p-3 text-muted small",
        "No deficit_pct data in the selected period."))
    # Summary stats
    med_pct  <- round(median(valid$deficit_pct, na.rm = TRUE), 1)
    mean_pct <- round(mean(valid$deficit_pct, na.rm = TRUE), 1)
    n_neg    <- sum(valid$deficit_pct < 0, na.rm = TRUE)
    last_val <- tail(valid$deficit_pct, 1)
    status_colour <- if (n_neg > 0) "#f08080" else if (abs(med_pct) > 20) "#ffd93d" else "#69d4a0"
    summary_ui <- tags$table(class = "table table-sm table-dark mt-2 mb-2",
      tags$thead(tags$tr(tags$th("Metric"), tags$th("Value"))),
      tags$tbody(
        tags$tr(tags$td("Median deficit_pct"), tags$td(sprintf("%.1f%%", med_pct))),
        tags$tr(tags$td("Mean deficit_pct"),   tags$td(sprintf("%.1f%%", mean_pct))),
        tags$tr(tags$td("Last observation"),   tags$td(sprintf("%.1f%%", last_val))),
        tags$tr(tags$td("Days deficit < 0 (anomaly)"),
                tags$td(style = paste0("color:", status_colour),
                        as.character(n_neg)))
      )
    )
    # Sparkline of deficit_pct over time
    dates_val <- valid$date
    vals_val  <- round(valid$deficit_pct, 1)
    spark <- ec_line(dates_val,
      list(list(name = "deficit_pct", type = "line",
                data = I(vals_val),
                itemStyle = list(color = "#4ea8de"),
                markLine = list(data = list(list(yAxis = 0,
                  lineStyle = list(color = "#f08080", type = "dashed")))))),
      h = "200px", y_name = "deficit %",
      aria_label = "Line chart: CodexBar vs ccusage deficit % over time")
    tagList(summary_ui, spark)
  })

  # --- Cross-check (ccusage) accordion renders (3b) ---------------------------
  # These are legacy validation charts; primary charts now use CodexBar (cb_d).
  output$cc_daily_cost_xcheck <- renderUI({
    d <- cc_d()
    if (!has_rows(d) || !"totalCost" %in% names(d))
      return(ec_empty("No ccusage daily cost data available"))
    agg <- aggregate(totalCost ~ date, data = d, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
    agg <- agg[order(agg$date), ]
    ec_line(agg$date,
      list(list(name = "ccusage (cross-check)", type = "line",
                data = I(round(agg$totalCost, 4)),
                itemStyle = list(color = SOURCE_PALETTE[["claude"]]),
                lineStyle  = list(type = "dashed"))),
      h = "250px", y_name = "Cost (USD)",
      aria_label = "Cross-check: ccusage daily Claude cost from legacy_ccusage_daily.json")
  })

  output$cc_weekly_model_xcheck <- renderUI({
    d <- cc_d()
    if (!has_rows(d) || is.null(d$modelsUsed)) return(ec_empty())
    models_split <- strsplit(as.character(d$modelsUsed), ",\\s*")
    n_each <- lengths(models_split)
    d_exp <- d[rep(seq_len(nrow(d)), n_each), ]
    d_exp$model <- clean_model(unlist(models_split))
    d_exp <- d_exp[d_exp$model != "", ]
    d_exp$week <- floor_date(d_exp$date, "week")
    agg <- aggregate(totalCost ~ week + model, data = d_exp, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
    all_weeks <- sort(unique(agg$week))
    models <- unique(agg$model)
    series <- lapply(models, function(m) {
      md <- agg[agg$model == m, ]
      vals <- rep(0, length(all_weeks))
      vals[match(md$week, all_weeks)] <- md$totalCost
      list(name = m, type = "line", data = I(round(vals, 2)))
    })
    ec_line(all_weeks, series, h = "250px",
            aria_label = "Cross-check: ccusage weekly cost by model from legacy_ccusage_daily.json")
  })

  # --- Reviews tab: roborev pipeline health -----------------------------------
  # Data loaded from roborev_summary.json (pre-computed by export_dashboard_data.R).
  # Falls back gracefully when the file is not available (no roborev data yet).
  roborev_summary_raw <- load_json("roborev_summary.json")
  # roborev_by_repo.json — per-repo high-severity counts for Panel 1 of #146.
  # Contract: [{"repo":"llm","n_total":120,"n_high":40,"n_resolved":80,"n_open":40}, ...]
  # Produced by the parallel exclusion PR; degrade gracefully when absent.
  roborev_by_repo_raw <- load_json("roborev_by_repo.json")
  # roborev_resolution.json — resolution rate KPI + weekly trend (#146 Q5).
  # Contract: {overall:{total,resolved,open,resolution_rate}, weekly:[{week_start,resolved,open,total,resolution_rate},...]}
  roborev_resolution_raw <- load_json("roborev_resolution.json")
  # roborev_agent_perf.json — per-agent pass rate + review counts (#146 Q10).
  # Contract: {agents:[{agent,n_runs,pass_count,fail_count,error_count,pass_rate,...}], generated_at}
  roborev_agent_perf_raw <- load_json("roborev_agent_perf.json")
  # roborev_backlog_age.json — open-review age distribution (#146 Q15).
  # Contract: {generated_at, n_open, median_hours, p90_hours, max_hours,
  #   buckets:[{label,hours_min,hours_max,n},...], top_oldest:[{repo,hours_open},...]}
  # Source: ~/.roborev/reviews.db (open reviews × enqueued_at), refreshed locally.
  roborev_backlog_age_raw <- load_json("roborev_backlog_age.json")
  # roborev_repo_signal.json — per-repo signal-to-noise ratio (#146 Q3).
  # Contract: {repos:[{repo,n_created,n_passed,n_failed,n_autoclosed,n_parse_fail,
  #   signal_ratio,noise_ratio},...], generated_at}
  roborev_repo_signal_raw <- load_json("roborev_repo_signal.json")
  # roborev_noise_rate.json — daily review outcome breakdown (#146 Q2).
  # Contract: {daily:[{date,passed,actioned_fail,autoclosed_fail,parse_fail,total},...],
  #   cumulative:{total,passed,actioned_fail,autoclosed_fail,parse_fail,noise_pct},
  #   generated_at}
  roborev_noise_rate_raw <- load_json("roborev_noise_rate.json")
  # roborev_suppression.json — medium-threshold autoclose count (#146 Q7).
  # Contract: {total_suppressed, period_start, top_repos:[{repo,autoclosed_severity,
  #   n_created,suppress_pct},...], generated_at}
  roborev_suppression_raw <- load_json("roborev_suppression.json")
  # roborev_cost_per_finding.json — cost per finding resolved (#146 Q1).
  # Contract: {series:[{agent,date,pass_count,addressed_count,cost_usd_per_finding}],
  #   summary:[{agent,total_pass,total_addressed,cost_usd_per_finding}],
  #   cost_instrumented:bool, generated_at}
  roborev_cpf_raw <- load_json("roborev_cost_per_finding.json")
  # roborev_cost_per_agent.json — cheapest agent per resolved finding (#146 Q11).
  # Contract: {agents:[{agent,total_pass,total_addressed,cost_usd_per_finding}],
  #   cost_instrumented:bool, generated_at}
  roborev_cpa_raw <- load_json("roborev_cost_per_agent.json")
  # roborev_vs_session_cost.json — session cost vs review cost (#146 Q20).
  # Contract: {daily:[{date,session_cost_usd,review_cost_usd,review_pct}],
  #   overall:{session_total,review_total,review_pct}, cost_instrumented:bool,
  #   note:str, generated_at}
  roborev_vsr_raw <- load_json("roborev_vs_session_cost.json")
  # roborev_commit_findings.json — top-N commits by finding count (#146 Q16).
  # Contract: {commits:[{commit_sha_short,commit_sha_full,repo,commit_msg,
  #   n_findings,last_reviewed},...], generated_at}
  roborev_commit_findings_raw <- load_json("roborev_commit_findings.json")
  # roborev_session_agent_noise.json — per-agent noise rate (#146 Q19).
  # Contract: {agents:[{agent,n_reviews,n_addressed,n_passed,n_autoclosed,n_parse_fail,
  #   noise_rate,pass_rate},...], generated_at}
  roborev_san_raw <- load_json("roborev_session_agent_noise.json")
  # roborev_session_findings.json — session duration vs roborev findings (#255 + #146 Q18).
  # Contract: [{session_id, duration_hours, n_findings, agent}, ...]
  # Source: sessions.parquet x git_commits.parquet x roborev_review_lifecycle.
  roborev_sf_raw <- load_json("roborev_session_findings.json")
  # roborev_location_findings.json — top-N files by finding frequency (#146 Q17).
  # Contract: {files:[{file_path,occurrences,distinct_repos,last_seen,n_high,n_medium,n_low}],
  #   n_reviews:int, generated_at}
  roborev_rlf_raw <- load_json("roborev_location_findings.json")
  # roborev_fix_timing.json — time from fix commit to review closure (#273 Q4).
  # Contract: {rows:[{fix_method,hours_fix_to_close}], n_fix_linked, n_manual,
  #   n_commit_ref, generated_at}
  roborev_rft_raw <- load_json("roborev_fix_timing.json")
  # roborev_closure_breakdown.json — closure reason split (#273 Q9).
  # Contract: {buckets:[{bucket,label,n}], n_closed, n_fix_linked, n_autoclose,
  #   n_unknown, fix_linked_pct, autoclose_pct, unknown_pct, generated_at}
  roborev_rcb_raw <- load_json("roborev_closure_breakdown.json")
  # roborev_loop_funnel.json — closing-the-loop funnel (#273 Q14).
  # Contract: {steps:[{step,label,n}], n_total, n_closed, n_fix_any,
  #   n_commit_ref, generated_at}
  roborev_rlf2_raw <- load_json("roborev_loop_funnel.json")

  rv_pulse <- if (!is.null(roborev_summary_raw) && is.list(roborev_summary_raw)) {
    roborev_summary_raw$pulse
  } else {
    NULL
  }
  rv_loops_raw <- if (!is.null(roborev_summary_raw) && is.list(roborev_summary_raw)) {
    as_df(roborev_summary_raw$loops)
  } else {
    NULL
  }

  safe_rv <- function(key, default = "—") {
    if (is.null(rv_pulse) || !key %in% names(rv_pulse)) return(default)
    v <- rv_pulse[[key]]
    if (is.null(v) || is.na(v)) default else as.character(v)
  }

  output$rv_n_open     <- renderText(safe_rv("n_open"))
  output$rv_n_critical <- renderText(safe_rv("n_critical"))
  output$rv_n_high     <- renderText(safe_rv("n_high"))
  output$rv_n_resolved <- renderText(safe_rv("n_resolved"))
  output$rv_n_loops    <- renderText(safe_rv("n_loops"))
  output$rv_n_escalate <- renderText(safe_rv("n_escalate"))
  output$rv_wasted_usd <- renderText({
    v <- safe_rv("wasted_usd", default = NA)
    if (is.na(v)) return("—")
    tryCatch(sprintf("$%.2f", as.numeric(v)), error = function(e) "—")
  })

  # Panel 1 of #146: per-repo high-severity findings — Cleveland dot chart.
  # Reads roborev_by_repo.json. Degrades to "no data yet" message when absent.
  output$rv_by_repo_chart <- renderUI({
    d <- as_df(roborev_by_repo_raw)
    if (!has_rows(d) || !"repo" %in% names(d))
      return(ec_empty("No per-repo roborev data yet — roborev_by_repo.json not deployed", h = "300px"))
    # Use n_high if present, else fall back to n_total
    val_col <- if ("n_high" %in% names(d)) "n_high" else if ("n_total" %in% names(d)) "n_total" else NULL
    if (is.null(val_col))
      return(ec_empty("roborev_by_repo.json missing n_high/n_total columns", h = "300px"))
    # Sort ascending (Cleveland dot: lowest at bottom, highest at top)
    d <- d[order(d[[val_col]], decreasing = FALSE), ]
    axis_label <- if (val_col == "n_high") "High-severity findings" else "Total findings"
    ec(list(
      tooltip = list(trigger = "axis", axisPointer = list(type = "shadow")),
      grid = list(left = 120, containLabel = FALSE),
      xAxis = list(type = "value", name = axis_label,
                   nameLocation = "middle", nameGap = 25,
                   axisLabel = list(color = "#ccc"),
                   nameTextStyle = list(color = "#ccc")),
      yAxis = list(type = "category", data = I(d$repo),
                   axisLabel = list(color = "#ccc", fontSize = 11)),
      series = list(list(
        type = "scatter",
        data = I(as.numeric(d[[val_col]])),
        symbolSize = 12,
        itemStyle = list(color = "#f08080")   # danger red from accessibility palette
      ))
    ), h = "300px",
    aria_label = paste0("Cleveland dot chart: ", tolower(axis_label), " per repository, sorted descending"))
  })

  output$rv_loops_table <- renderDT({
    if (!has_rows(rv_loops_raw)) {
      return(datatable(data.frame(message = "No loop data available"),
                       options = list(dom = "t"), rownames = FALSE))
    }
    d <- rv_loops_raw
    if ("cycles" %in% names(d)) d <- d[order(-as.integer(d$cycles)), ]
    cols <- intersect(
      c("tier", "cycles", "severity", "primary_file", "summary",
        "first_seen", "last_seen", "estimated_wasted_usd"),
      names(d)
    )
    d <- d[, cols, drop = FALSE]
    row_js <- DT::JS("function(row, data) {
      var tier = data[0];
      var palette = {escalate: '#ff5555', block: '#ffaa3d', watch: '#ffd93d'};
      var bg = palette[tier];
      if (bg) $('td', row).css({'background-color': bg, 'color': '#000000'});
    }")
    datatable(d, rownames = FALSE,
      options = list(
        order      = list(list(1, "desc")),
        pageLength = 25,
        dom        = "frtip",
        rowCallback = row_js,
        scrollX    = TRUE
      )
    )
  })

  # --- Resolution Rate panel (#146 Q5) -----------------------------------------
  # KPI text outputs from roborev_resolution.json overall block.
  safe_res <- function(key, fmt = "%s", default = "—") {
    if (is.null(roborev_resolution_raw) || !is.list(roborev_resolution_raw)) return(default)
    ov <- roborev_resolution_raw$overall
    if (is.null(ov) || !key %in% names(ov)) return(default)
    v <- ov[[key]]
    if (is.null(v) || (length(v) == 1 && is.na(v))) return(default)
    tryCatch(sprintf(fmt, v), error = function(e) default)
  }

  output$rv_res_rate <- renderText({
    v <- safe_res("resolution_rate")
    if (v == "—") return("—")
    rate_num <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(v))
    if (is.na(rate_num)) return("—")
    sprintf("%.1f%%", rate_num * 100)
  })
  output$rv_res_resolved <- renderText(safe_res("resolved", "%d"))
  output$rv_res_open     <- renderText(safe_res("open",     "%d"))
  output$rv_res_total    <- renderText(safe_res("total",    "%d"))

  output$rv_resolution_trend <- renderUI({
    if (is.null(roborev_resolution_raw) || !is.list(roborev_resolution_raw)) {
      return(ec_empty("roborev_resolution.json not yet available", h = "300px"))
    }
    weekly_raw <- roborev_resolution_raw$weekly
    weekly_empty <- is.null(weekly_raw) ||
      (is.data.frame(weekly_raw) && nrow(weekly_raw) == 0L) ||
      (!is.data.frame(weekly_raw) && length(weekly_raw) == 0L)
    if (weekly_empty) {
      return(ec_empty("No weekly resolution trend data yet", h = "300px"))
    }
    # load_json() uses fromJSON(simplifyDataFrame = TRUE): a JSON array of objects
    # arrives as a data.frame. Accept either a data.frame or a list-of-lists.
    wdf <- if (is.data.frame(weekly_raw)) {
      weekly_raw
    } else {
      do.call(rbind, lapply(weekly_raw, function(r) {
        data.frame(
          week_start      = as.character(r$week_start),
          resolved        = as.numeric(r$resolved),
          open            = as.numeric(r$open),
          total           = as.numeric(r$total),
          resolution_rate = as.numeric(r$resolution_rate),
          stringsAsFactors = FALSE
        )
      }))
    }
    # Coerce to expected types (data.frame branch may carry integer/character cols).
    wdf$week_start      <- as.character(wdf$week_start)
    wdf$resolved        <- as.numeric(wdf$resolved)
    wdf$open            <- as.numeric(wdf$open)
    wdf <- wdf[order(wdf$week_start), ]

    # Line chart: resolved vs open per week (NOT a bar/stacked bar per viz rules)
    # Range slider defaults to last 3 months (~13 weeks)
    total_weeks <- nrow(wdf)
    dz_start <- if (total_weeks > 13) round(100 * (1 - 13 / total_weeks)) else 0

    ec(list(
      tooltip  = list(trigger = "axis"),
      legend   = list(bottom = 30, textStyle = list(color = "#ccc"),
                      data = list("Resolved", "Open")),
      toolbox  = list(feature = list(
        dataZoom = list(yAxisIndex = "none"),
        restore  = list(),
        saveAsImage = list()
      )),
      dataZoom = list(
        list(type = "inside", start = dz_start, end = 100, filterMode = "filter"),
        list(type = "slider", start = dz_start, end = 100, bottom = 5, filterMode = "filter")
      ),
      grid     = list(containLabel = TRUE, bottom = 80),
      xAxis    = list(type = "category", data = I(wdf$week_start),
                      axisLabel = list(rotate = 30, color = "#ccc"),
                      name = "Week start (Monday)", nameLocation = "middle", nameGap = 45,
                      nameTextStyle = list(color = "#ccc")),
      yAxis    = list(type = "value", name = "Review count",
                      nameTextStyle = list(color = "#ccc"),
                      axisLabel = list(color = "#ccc")),
      series   = list(
        list(name = "Resolved", type = "line", data = I(wdf$resolved),
             smooth = FALSE, itemStyle = list(color = "#69d4a0"),
             lineStyle = list(color = "#69d4a0", width = 2)),
        list(name = "Open", type = "line", data = I(wdf$open),
             smooth = FALSE, itemStyle = list(color = "#f08080"),
             lineStyle = list(color = "#f08080", width = 2))
      )
    ), h = "300px",
    aria_label = paste0(
      "Line chart: roborev resolved vs open reviews per week. ",
      "Green = resolved, red = open. Defaulting to last 3 months view."
    ))
  })

  # --- Signal-to-Noise by Repo panel (#146 Q3) ---------------------------------
  # Cleveland dot chart: signal_ratio per repo, sorted ascending (lowest at bottom).
  # Compact table: repo | signal | noise | created | passed | failed | autoclosed.
  # Source: roborev_repo_signal.json {repos:[{repo,signal_ratio,noise_ratio,...}]}.
  #
  # Named colour palette — same repo gets same colour across all panels (per
  # narrative-colour-persistence rule). Using project palette from accessibility rule.
  REPO_PALETTE <- c(
    "llm"              = "#4ea8de",
    "llmtelemetry"     = "#69d4a0",
    "historical"       = "#ffd93d",
    "crypto_swarms"    = "#c084fc",
    "irishbuoys"       = "#5edaff",
    "irish_buoy_network" = "#5edaff",
    "footbet"          = "#f08080",
    "football"         = "#f08080",
    "randomwalk"       = "#ffaa3d",
    "mycare"           = "#98d8c8",
    "knowledge"        = "#b0c4de"
  )

  safe_signal_repos <- function(raw) {
    if (is.null(raw) || !is.list(raw)) return(NULL)
    repos <- raw$repos
    if (is.null(repos)) return(NULL)
    df <- if (is.data.frame(repos)) {
      if (nrow(repos) == 0L) return(NULL)
      repos
    } else if (length(repos) > 0L) {
      do.call(rbind, lapply(repos, function(r) {
        data.frame(
          repo         = as.character(r$repo),
          n_created    = as.integer(r$n_created),
          n_passed     = as.integer(r$n_passed),
          n_failed     = as.integer(r$n_failed),
          n_autoclosed = as.integer(if (!is.null(r$n_autoclosed)) r$n_autoclosed else 0L),
          n_parse_fail = as.integer(if (!is.null(r$n_parse_fail)) r$n_parse_fail else 0L),
          signal_ratio = as.numeric(r$signal_ratio),
          noise_ratio  = as.numeric(r$noise_ratio),
          stringsAsFactors = FALSE
        )
      }))
    } else {
      return(NULL)
    }
    # Normalise column types from data.frame branch
    data.frame(
      repo         = as.character(df$repo),
      n_created    = as.integer(df$n_created),
      n_passed     = as.integer(df$n_passed),
      n_failed     = as.integer(df$n_failed),
      n_autoclosed = if ("n_autoclosed" %in% names(df)) as.integer(df$n_autoclosed) else 0L,
      n_parse_fail = if ("n_parse_fail" %in% names(df)) as.integer(df$n_parse_fail) else 0L,
      signal_ratio = as.numeric(df$signal_ratio),
      noise_ratio  = as.numeric(df$noise_ratio),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  }

  output$rv_repo_signal_chart <- renderUI({
    sig <- safe_signal_repos(roborev_repo_signal_raw)
    if (is.null(sig))
      return(ec_empty(
        "No signal-to-noise data yet — roborev_repo_signal.json not deployed", h = "300px"))
    # Sort ascending for Cleveland dot (lowest at bottom, highest at top)
    sig <- sig[order(sig$signal_ratio, decreasing = FALSE), ]
    n_repos   <- nrow(sig)
    top_repo  <- sig$repo[n_repos]
    top_rate  <- round(sig$signal_ratio[n_repos] * 100, 1)
    # Assign colours from REPO_PALETTE; fall back to a neutral grey for unknown repos.
    colours <- vapply(sig$repo, function(r) {
      if (r %in% names(REPO_PALETTE)) REPO_PALETTE[[r]] else "#888888"
    }, character(1))
    # Build ECharts scatter series with per-point colour
    point_data <- lapply(seq_len(n_repos), function(i) {
      list(
        value     = sig$signal_ratio[i],
        itemStyle = list(color = colours[i])
      )
    })
    ec(list(
      tooltip = list(
        trigger = "axis",
        axisPointer = list(type = "shadow"),
        formatter = DT::JS(
          "function(params) {",
          "  var d = params[0];",
          "  return d.name + '<br/>Signal: ' + (d.value * 100).toFixed(1) + '%';",
          "}"
        )
      ),
      grid = list(left = 130, containLabel = FALSE),
      xAxis = list(
        type = "value",
        name = "Signal ratio (passed / created)",
        nameLocation = "middle", nameGap = 28,
        min = 0, max = 1,
        axisLabel = list(
          color = "#ccc",
          formatter = DT::JS("function(v){ return (v*100).toFixed(0)+'%'; }")
        ),
        nameTextStyle = list(color = "#ccc")
      ),
      yAxis = list(
        type = "category",
        data = I(sig$repo),
        axisLabel = list(color = "#ccc", fontSize = 11)
      ),
      series = list(list(
        type       = "scatter",
        data       = I(point_data),
        symbolSize = 14
      ))
    ), h = paste0(max(280, n_repos * 28), "px"),
    aria_label = paste0(
      "Cleveland dot chart: roborev signal-to-noise ratio per repository. ",
      n_repos, " repos. Best signal: ", top_repo, " at ", top_rate, "%. ",
      "X-axis shows signal ratio (reviews passed / reviews created)."
    ))
  })

  output$rv_repo_signal_table <- renderUI({
    sig <- safe_signal_repos(roborev_repo_signal_raw)
    if (is.null(sig)) {
      return(tags$tr(tags$td(colspan = "7", "No data available", class = "text-muted")))
    }
    # Sort by signal_ratio descending for table view
    sig <- sig[order(sig$signal_ratio, decreasing = TRUE), ]
    rows <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(sig)), function(i) {
      r <- sig[i, , drop = FALSE]
      sig_pct   <- sprintf("%.1f%%", r$signal_ratio * 100)
      noise_pct <- sprintf("%.1f%%", r$noise_ratio  * 100)
      # Colour-code signal cell: green >= 30%, yellow 10–30%, red < 10%
      sig_style <- if (r$signal_ratio >= 0.30) "color:#69d4a0;font-weight:bold"
                   else if (r$signal_ratio >= 0.10) "color:#ffd93d"
                   else "color:#f08080"
      tags$tr(
        tags$td(r$repo),
        tags$td(style = sig_style, sig_pct),
        tags$td(noise_pct),
        tags$td(as.character(r$n_created)),
        tags$td(as.character(r$n_passed)),
        tags$td(as.character(r$n_failed)),
        tags$td(as.character(r$n_autoclosed))
      )
    })
    tagList(rows)
  })

  # --- Cadence Reduction Effect panel (#146 Q6) --------------------------------
  # Before/after split at 2026-05-21 (llm#217 cadence reduction).
  # KPI table: period | created | passed | pass_rate | autoclosed | days.
  # Two-segment line chart: daily volume (left axis) + pass rate (right axis).
  # Source: roborev_cadence_efficacy.json.
  roborev_cadence_efficacy_raw <- load_json("roborev_cadence_efficacy.json")
